    39	var _ plugins.Scaffolder = &webhookScaffolder{}
    40	
    41	type webhookScaffolder struct {
    42	        config   config.Config
    43	        resource resource.Resource
    44	
    45	        // fs is the filesystem that will be used by the scaffolder
    46	        fs machinery.Filesystem
    47	
    48	        // force indicates whether to scaffold controller files even if it exists or not
    49	        force bool
    50	
    51	        // Deprecated - TODO: remove it for go/v5
    52	        // isLegacy indicates that the resource should be created in the legacy path under the api
    53	        isLegacy bool
    54	}
    55	
    56	// NewWebhookScaffolder returns a new Scaffolder for v2 webhook creation operations
    57	func NewWebhookScaffolder(cfg config.Config, res resource.Resource, force bool, isLegacy bool) plugins.Scaffolder {
    58	        return &webhookScaffolder{
    59	                config:   cfg,
    60	                resource: res,
    61	                force:    force,
    62	                isLegacy: isLegacy,
    63	        }
    64	}
    65	
    66	// InjectFS implements cmdutil.Scaffolder
    67	func (s *webhookScaffolder) InjectFS(fs machinery.Filesystem) {
    68	        s.fs = fs
    69	}
    70	
    71	// Scaffold implements cmdutil.Scaffolder
    72	func (s *webhookScaffolder) Scaffold() error {
    73	        log.Info("Writing scaffold for you to edit...")
    74	
    75	        // Load the boilerplate
    76	        boilerplate, err := afero.ReadFile(s.fs.FS, hack.DefaultBoilerplatePath)
    77	        if err != nil {
    78	                if errors.Is(err, afero.ErrFileNotFound) {
    79	                        log.Warn("unable to find boilerplate file. "+
    80	                                "This file is used to generate the license header in the project..\n"+
    81	                                "Note that controller-gen will also use this. Ensure that you "+
    82	                                "add the license file or configure your project accordingly",
    83	                                "file_path", hack.DefaultBoilerplatePath, "error", err)
    84	                        boilerplate = []byte("")
    85	                } else {
    86	                        return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding webhook: failed to load boilerplate: %w", err)
    87	                }
    88	        }
    89	
    90	        // Initialize the machinery.Scaffold that will write the files to disk
    91	        scaffold := machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
    92	                machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
    93	                machinery.WithBoilerplate(string(boilerplate)),
    94	                machinery.WithResource(&s.resource),
    95	        )
    96	
    97	        // Keep track of these values before the update
    98	        doDefaulting := s.resource.HasDefaultingWebhook()
    99	        doValidation := s.resource.HasValidationWebhook()
   100	        doConversion := s.resource.HasConversionWebhook()
   101	
   102	        if err = s.config.UpdateResource(s.resource); err != nil {
   103	                return fmt.Errorf("error updating resource: %w", err)
   104	        }
   105	
   106	        // Check if webhook files exist
   107	        webhookFilePath := s.getWebhookFilePath()
   108	        webhookFileExists := false
   109	        if _, statErr := s.fs.FS.Stat(webhookFilePath); statErr == nil {
   110	                webhookFileExists = true
   111	        }
   112	
   113	        webhookTestFilePath := s.getWebhookTestFilePath()
   114	        webhookTestFileExists := false
   115	        if _, statErr := s.fs.FS.Stat(webhookTestFilePath); statErr == nil {
   116	                webhookTestFileExists = true
   117	        }
   118	
   119	        // Scaffold or update webhook file (for all webhook types)
   120	        // Note: Conversion webhooks also need a webhook.go file with minimal setup (.For(&Type{}).Complete())
   121	        // This is how controller-runtime discovers Hub/Convertible interfaces
   122	        if doDefaulting || doValidation || doConversion {
   123	                if err = s.scaffoldWebhookFile(scaffold, webhookFileExists); err != nil {
   124	                        return err
   125	                }
   126	
   127	                // Update main.go to wire webhook setup function (for all webhook types)
   128	                if err = scaffold.Execute(
   129	                        &cmd.MainUpdater{WireWebhook: true, IsLegacyPath: s.isLegacy},
   130	                ); err != nil {
   131	                        return fmt.Errorf("error updating main.go: %w", err)
   132	                }
   133	        }
   134	
   135	        // Scaffold or update webhook test file (for all webhook types)
   136	        if err = s.scaffoldWebhookTestFile(scaffold, webhookTestFileExists); err != nil {
   137	                return err
   138	        }
   139	
   140	        // Update e2e tests
   141	        // WireWebhook controls webhook service readiness checks (for defaulting/validation)
   142	        // But conversion webhooks still need CA injection tests (handled inside updater)
   143	        if err = scaffold.Execute(
   144	                &e2e.WebhookTestUpdater{WireWebhook: doDefaulting || doValidation},
   145	        ); err != nil {
   146	                return fmt.Errorf("error updating e2e tests: %w", err)
   147	        }
   148	
   149	        if doConversion {
   150	                // Update the types file to add storage version marker
   151	                if err = scaffold.Execute(&api.TypesUpdater{}); err != nil {
   152	                        return fmt.Errorf("error updating types file with storage version marker: %w", err)
   153	                }
   154	
   155	                if err = scaffold.Execute(&api.Hub{Force: s.force}); err != nil {
   156	                        return fmt.Errorf("error scaffold resource with hub: %w", err)
   157	                }
   158	
   159	                for _, spoke := range s.resource.Webhooks.Spoke {
   160	                        log.Info("Scaffolding for spoke version", "version", spoke)
   161	                        if err = scaffold.Execute(&api.Spoke{Force: s.force, SpokeVersion: spoke}); err != nil {
   162	                                return fmt.Errorf("failed to scaffold spoke %s: %w", spoke, err)
   163	                        }
   164	                }
   165	
   166	                log.Info(`Webhook server has been set up for you.
   167	You need to implement the conversion.Hub and conversion.Convertible interfaces for your CRD types.`)
   168	        }
   169	
   170	        // Scaffold webhook suite test for all webhook types
   171	        // Note: Conversion webhooks also need the suite to register with envtest
   172	        if doDefaulting || doValidation || doConversion {
   173	                if err = scaffold.Execute(&webhooks.WebhookSuite{IsLegacyPath: s.isLegacy}); err != nil {
   174	                        return fmt.Errorf("error scaffold webhook suite: %w", err)
   175	                }
   176	        }
   177	
   178	        // TODO: remove for go/v5
   179	        if !s.isLegacy {
   180	                if hasInternalController, err := pluginutil.HasFileContentWith("Dockerfile", "internal/controller"); err != nil {
   181	                        log.Error("failed to read Dockerfile to check if webhook(s) will be properly copied", "error", err)
   182	                } else if hasInternalController {
   183	                        log.Warn("Dockerfile is copying internal/controller; to allow copying webhooks, " +
   184	                                "it will be edited, and `internal/controller` will be replaced by `internal/`")
   185	
   186	                        if err = pluginutil.ReplaceInFile("Dockerfile", "internal/controller", "internal/"); err != nil {
   187	                                log.Error("failed to replace \"internal/controller\" with \"internal/\" in the Dockerfile", "error", err)
   188	                        }
   189	                }
   190	        }
   191	        return nil
   192	}
   193	
   194	// getWebhookFilePath returns the path to the webhook file
   195	func (s *webhookScaffolder) getWebhookFilePath() string {
   196	        baseDir := "api"
   197	        if !s.isLegacy {
   198	                baseDir = "internal/webhook"
   199	        }
   200	
   201	        var path string
   202	        if s.config.IsMultiGroup() && s.resource.Group != "" {
   203	                path = fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s/%s_webhook.go",
   204	                        baseDir, s.resource.Group, s.resource.Version, strings.ToLower(s.resource.Kind))
   205	        } else {
   206	                path = fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s_webhook.go",
   207	                        baseDir, s.resource.Version, strings.ToLower(s.resource.Kind))
   208	        }
   209	
   210	        return path
   211	}
   212	
   213	// getWebhookTestFilePath returns the path to the webhook test file
   214	func (s *webhookScaffolder) getWebhookTestFilePath() string {
   215	        baseDir := "api"
   216	        if !s.isLegacy {
   217	                baseDir = "internal/webhook"
   218	        }
   219	
   220	        var path string
   221	        if s.config.IsMultiGroup() && s.resource.Group != "" {
   222	                path = fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s/%s_webhook_test.go",
   223	                        baseDir, s.resource.Group, s.resource.Version, strings.ToLower(s.resource.Kind))
   224	        } else {
   225	                path = fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s_webhook_test.go",
   226	                        baseDir, s.resource.Version, strings.ToLower(s.resource.Kind))
   227	        }
   228	
   229	        return path
   230	}
   231	
   232	// scaffoldWebhookFile creates or updates the webhook implementation file
   233	func (s *webhookScaffolder) scaffoldWebhookFile(scaffold *machinery.Scaffold, fileExists bool) error {
   234	        if !fileExists || s.force {
   235	                if err := scaffold.Execute(
   236	                        &webhooks.Webhook{Force: s.force, IsLegacyPath: s.isLegacy},
   237	                ); err != nil {
   238	                        return fmt.Errorf("error creating webhook: %w", err)
   239	                }
   240	        } else if fileExists && !s.force && !s.isLegacy {
   241	                log.Info("Adding new webhook type to existing file")
   242	                if err := scaffold.Execute(
   243	                        &webhooks.WebhookUpdater{},
   244	                ); err != nil {
   245	                        return fmt.Errorf("error updating webhook: %w", err)
   246	                }
   247	        }
   248	        return nil
   249	}
   250	
   251	// scaffoldWebhookTestFile creates or updates the webhook test file
   252	func (s *webhookScaffolder) scaffoldWebhookTestFile(scaffold *machinery.Scaffold, fileExists bool) error {
   253	        if !fileExists || s.force {
   254	                if err := scaffold.Execute(
   255	                        &webhooks.WebhookTest{Force: s.force, IsLegacyPath: s.isLegacy},
   256	                ); err != nil {
   257	                        return fmt.Errorf("error creating webhook test: %w", err)
   258	                }
   259	        } else if fileExists && !s.force && !s.isLegacy {
   260	                if err := scaffold.Execute(
   261	                        &webhooks.WebhookTestUpdater{},
   262	                ); err != nil {
   263	                        return fmt.Errorf("error updating webhook test: %w", err)
   264	                }
   265	        }
   266	        return nil
   267	}
   268

Now let me look at how the `year` parameter is used in controller-gen's PR description. Let me also check what changes need to be made.

Let me summarize what needs to be done based on the PR description:

1. **Scaffold `YEAR` instead of a resolved year**: The `hack/boilerplate.go.txt` should contain `Copyright YEAR The Kubernetes authors.` instead of a hardcoded year.

2. **Substitute `YEAR` when scaffolding files**: When kubebuilder reads `boilerplate.go.txt` from disk before scaffolding new files (e.g., `kubebuilder create api`), it replaces `YEAR` with the current year.

   ```makefile
   YEAR ?= $(shell date +%Y)
   ...
   "$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object:headerFile="hack/boilerplate.go.txt",year=$(YEAR) paths="./..."
   ```

    23	        "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/config"
    24	        "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugin"
    25	)
    26	
    27	var _ = Describe("init", func() {
    28	        Context("getInitHelpExamples", func() {
    29	                It("should return help examples for available project versions", func() {
    30	                        plugin1 := newMockPlugin("test.plugin", "3", config.Version{Number: 3})
    31	                        plugin2 := newMockPlugin("another.plugin", "3", config.Version{Number: 3}, config.Version{Number: 4})
    32	
    33	                        cli := CLI{
    34	                                commandName: "kubebuilder",
    35	                                plugins: map[string]plugin.Plugin{
    36	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin1): plugin1,
    37	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin2): plugin2,
    38	                                },
    39	                        }
    40	
    41	                        examples := cli.getInitHelpExamples()
    42	
    43	                        Expect(examples).To(ContainSubstring("kubebuilder init"))
    44	                        Expect(examples).To(ContainSubstring("project-version"))
    45	                        Expect(examples).To(ContainSubstring("-h"))
    46	                })
    47	
    48	                It("should handle multiple versions", func() {
    49	                        plugin1 := newMockPlugin("plugin1", "3", config.Version{Number: 2})
    50	                        plugin2 := newMockPlugin("plugin2", "3", config.Version{Number: 3})
    51	
    52	                        cli := CLI{
    53	                                commandName: "kb",
    54	                                plugins: map[string]plugin.Plugin{
    55	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin1): plugin1,
    56	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin2): plugin2,
    57	                                },
    58	                        }
    59	
    60	                        examples := cli.getInitHelpExamples()
    61	
    62	                        Expect(examples).To(ContainSubstring("kb init"))
    63	                        Expect(examples).NotTo(HaveSuffix("\n\n"))
    64	                })
    65	
    66	                It("should return empty string when no plugins", func() {
    67	                        cli := CLI{
    68	                                commandName: "kubebuilder",
    69	                                plugins:     map[string]plugin.Plugin{},
    70	                        }
    71	
    72	                        examples := cli.getInitHelpExamples()
    73	                        Expect(examples).To(BeEmpty())
    74	                })
    75	        })
    76	
    77	        Context("getAvailableProjectVersions", func() {
    78	                It("should return unique project versions", func() {
    79	                        plugin1 := newMockPlugin("plugin1", "3", config.Version{Number: 3})
    80	                        plugin2 := newMockPlugin("plugin2", "3", config.Version{Number: 3})
    81	                        plugin3 := newMockPlugin("plugin3", "3", config.Version{Number: 4})
    82	
    83	                        cli := CLI{
    84	                                plugins: map[string]plugin.Plugin{
    85	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin1): plugin1,
    86	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin2): plugin2,
    87	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin3): plugin3,
    88	                                },
    89	                        }
    90	
    91	                        versions := cli.getAvailableProjectVersions()
    92	
    93	                        Expect(versions).To(ContainElement("\"3\""))
    94	                        Expect(versions).To(ContainElement("\"4\""))
    95	                        Expect(versions).To(HaveLen(2))
    96	                })
    97	
    98	                It("should exclude deprecated plugins", func() {
    99	                        deprecatedPlugin := newMockDeprecatedPlugin("deprecated", "3", "use v4 instead", config.Version{Number: 2})
   100	                        regularPlugin := newMockPlugin("regular", "3", config.Version{Number: 3})
   101	
   102	                        cli := CLI{
   103	                                plugins: map[string]plugin.Plugin{
   104	                                        plugin.KeyFor(deprecatedPlugin): deprecatedPlugin,
   105	                                        plugin.KeyFor(regularPlugin):    regularPlugin,
   106	                                },
   107	                        }
   108	
   109	                        versions := cli.getAvailableProjectVersions()
   110	
   111	                        Expect(versions).NotTo(ContainElement("\"2\""))
   112	                        Expect(versions).To(ContainElement("\"3\""))
   113	                })
   114	
   115	                It("should return sorted versions", func() {
   116	                        plugin1 := newMockPlugin("plugin1", "3", config.Version{Number: 4})
   117	                        plugin2 := newMockPlugin("plugin2", "3", config.Version{Number: 2})
   118	                        plugin3 := newMockPlugin("plugin3", "3", config.Version{Number: 3})
   119	
   120	                        cli := CLI{
   121	                                plugins: map[string]plugin.Plugin{
   122	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin1): plugin1,
   123	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin2): plugin2,
   124	                                        plugin.KeyFor(plugin3): plugin3,
   125	                                },
   126	                        }
   127	
   128	                        versions := cli.getAvailableProjectVersions()
   129	
   130	                        Expect(versions).To(Equal([]string{"\"2\"", "\"3\"", "\"4\""}))
   131	                })
   132	
   133	                It("should return empty slice when no plugins", func() {
   134	                        cli := CLI{
   135	                                plugins: map[string]plugin.Plugin{},
   136	                        }
   137	
   138	                        versions := cli.getAvailableProjectVersions()
   139	                        Expect(versions).To(BeEmpty())
   140	                })
   141	
   142	                It("should handle plugin with multiple supported versions", func() {
   143	                        multiPlugin := newMockPlugin("multi", "3",
   144	                                config.Version{Number: 2},
   145	                                config.Version{Number: 3},
   146	                                config.Version{Number: 4})
   147	
   148	                        cli := CLI{
   149	                                plugins: map[string]plugin.Plugin{
   150	                                        plugin.KeyFor(multiPlugin): multiPlugin,
   151	                                },
   152	                        }
   153	
   154	                        versions := cli.getAvailableProjectVersions()
   155	
   156	                        Expect(versions).To(HaveLen(3))
   157	                        Expect(versions).To(ContainElement("\"2\""))
   158	                        Expect(versions).To(ContainElement("\"3\""))
   159	                        Expect(versions).To(ContainElement("\"4\""))
   160	                })

    27	        cfgv3 "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/config/v3"
    28	)
    29	
    30	const testRepo = "github.com/example/test"
    31	
    32	var _ = Describe("initSubcommand", func() {
    33	        var (
    34	                subCmd *initSubcommand
    35	                cfg    config.Config
    36	        )
    37	
    38	        BeforeEach(func() {
    39	                subCmd = &initSubcommand{}
    40	                cfg = cfgv3.New()
    41	        })
    42	
    43	        Context("InjectConfig", func() {
    44	                It("should set repository when provided", func() {
    45	                        subCmd.repo = testRepo
    46	                        err := subCmd.InjectConfig(cfg)
    47	
    48	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    49	                        Expect(cfg.GetRepository()).To(Equal(testRepo))
    50	                })
    51	
    52	                It("should fail when repository cannot be detected", func() {
    53	                        originalDir, err := os.Getwd()
    54	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    55	                        defer func() { _ = os.Chdir(originalDir) }()
    56	
    57	                        tmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "test-init")
    58	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    59	                        defer func() { _ = os.RemoveAll(tmpDir) }()
    60	
    61	                        err = os.Chdir(tmpDir)
    62	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    63	
    64	                        subCmd.repo = ""
    65	                        err = subCmd.InjectConfig(cfg)
    66	
    67	                        Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
    68	                })
    69	
    70	                It("should set multigroup when flag is enabled", func() {
    71	                        subCmd.repo = testRepo
    72	                        subCmd.multigroup = true
    73	                        err := subCmd.InjectConfig(cfg)
    74	
    75	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    76	                        Expect(cfg.IsMultiGroup()).To(BeTrue())
    77	                })
    78	
    79	                It("should not set multigroup when flag is disabled", func() {
    80	                        subCmd.repo = testRepo
    81	                        subCmd.multigroup = false
    82	                        err := subCmd.InjectConfig(cfg)
    83	
    84	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    85	                        Expect(cfg.IsMultiGroup()).To(BeFalse())
    86	                })
    87	
    88	                It("should set namespaced when flag is enabled", func() {
    89	                        subCmd.repo = testRepo
    90	                        subCmd.namespaced = true
    91	                        err := subCmd.InjectConfig(cfg)
    92	
    93	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    94	                        Expect(cfg.IsNamespaced()).To(BeTrue())
    95	                })
    96	
    97	                It("should set both multigroup and namespaced when both flags are enabled", func() {
    98	                        subCmd.repo = testRepo
    99	                        subCmd.multigroup = true
   100	                        subCmd.namespaced = true
   101	                        err := subCmd.InjectConfig(cfg)
   102	
   103	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   104	                        Expect(cfg.IsMultiGroup()).To(BeTrue())
   105	                        Expect(cfg.IsNamespaced()).To(BeTrue())
   106	                })
   107	        })
   108	
   109	        Context("checkDir validation", func() {
   110	                var tmpDir string
   111	
   112	                BeforeEach(func() {
   113	                        var err error
   114	                        tmpDir, err = os.MkdirTemp("", "test-checkdir")
   115	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   116	
   117	                        originalDir, err := os.Getwd()
   118	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   119	                        DeferCleanup(func() {
   120	                                _ = os.Chdir(originalDir)
   121	                                _ = os.RemoveAll(tmpDir)
   122	                        })
   123	
   124	                        err = os.Chdir(tmpDir)
   125	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   126	                })
   127	
   128	                It("should pass for empty directory", func() {
   129	                        Expect(checkDir()).To(Succeed())
   130	                })
   131	
   132	                It("should pass when only go.mod exists", func() {
   133	                        err := os.WriteFile("go.mod", []byte("module test"), 0o644)
   134	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   135	
   136	                        Expect(checkDir()).To(Succeed())
   137	                })
   138	
   139	                It("should fail when PROJECT already exists", func() {
   140	                        err := os.WriteFile("PROJECT", []byte("version: 3"), 0o644)
   141	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   142	
   143	                        err = checkDir()
   144	                        Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
   145	                        Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring("already initialized"))
   146	                })
   147	
   148	                It("should fail when Makefile exists", func() {
   149	                        err := os.WriteFile("Makefile", []byte("all:"), 0o644)
   150	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   151	
   152	                        err = checkDir()
   153	                        Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
   154	                        Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring("already initialized"))
   155	                })
   156	
   157	                It("should fail when cmd/main.go exists", func() {
   158	                        err := os.MkdirAll("cmd", 0o755)
   159	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())

   161	                        err = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join("cmd", "main.go"), []byte("package main"), 0o644)

   163	
   164	                        err = checkDir()
   165	                        Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
   166	                        Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring("already initialized"))
   167	                })
   168	        })

    20	
    21	import (
    22	        "os"
    23	        "path/filepath"
    24	        "strings"
    25	
    26	        . "github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2"
    27	        . "github.com/onsi/gomega"
    28	
    29	        pluginutil "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugin/util"
    30	        "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/test/e2e/utils"
    31	)
    32	
    33	var _ = Describe("Init Scaffolding Integration Test", func() {
    34	        var kbc *utils.TestContext
    35	
    36	        BeforeEach(func() {
    37	                var err error
    38	                kbc, err = utils.NewTestContext(pluginutil.KubebuilderBinName, "GO111MODULE=on")
    39	                Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    40	                Expect(kbc.Prepare()).To(Succeed())
    41	        })
    42	
    43	        AfterEach(func() {
    44	                kbc.Destroy()
    45	        })
    46	
    47	        Context("cluster-scoped init (default)", func() {
    48	                It("should scaffold cluster-scoped configuration", func() {
    49	                        By("initializing a cluster-scoped project")
    50	                        err := kbc.Init(
    51	                                "--plugins", "go/v4",
    52	                                "--project-version", "3",
    53	                                "--domain", kbc.Domain,
    54	                        )
    55	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    56	
    57	                        By("verifying PROJECT file does not have namespaced flag")
    58	                        projectFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "PROJECT")
    59	                        content, err := os.ReadFile(projectFile)
    60	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    61	                        projectContent := string(content)
    62	                        // Check root level doesn't have namespaced: true
    63	                        beforeResources := strings.Split(projectContent, "resources:")[0]
    64	                        if strings.Contains(beforeResources, "namespaced:") {
    65	                                Expect(beforeResources).NotTo(ContainSubstring("namespaced: true"))
    66	                        }
    67	
    68	                        By("verifying RBAC is cluster-scoped")
    69	                        roleFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "rbac", "role.yaml")
    70	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(roleFile)
    71	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    72	                        Expect(string(content)).To(ContainSubstring("kind: ClusterRole"))
    73	
    74	                        roleBindingFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "rbac", "role_binding.yaml")
    75	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(roleBindingFile)
    76	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    77	                        Expect(string(content)).To(ContainSubstring("kind: ClusterRoleBinding"))
    78	
    79	                        By("verifying manager.yaml does NOT have WATCH_NAMESPACE")
    80	                        managerFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "manager", "manager.yaml")
    81	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(managerFile)
    82	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    83	                        Expect(string(content)).NotTo(ContainSubstring("WATCH_NAMESPACE"))
    84	
    85	                        By("verifying cmd/main.go does NOT have namespace helper functions")
    86	                        mainFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "cmd", "main.go")
    87	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(mainFile)
    88	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
    89	                        mainContent := string(content)
    90	                        Expect(mainContent).NotTo(ContainSubstring("func getWatchNamespace()"))
    91	                        Expect(mainContent).NotTo(ContainSubstring("func setupCacheNamespaces"))
    92	                })
    93	        })
    94	
    95	        Context("namespace-scoped init (--namespaced)", func() {
    96	                It("should scaffold namespace-scoped configuration", func() {
    97	                        By("initializing a namespace-scoped project")
    98	                        err := kbc.Init(
    99	                                "--plugins", "go/v4",
   100	                                "--project-version", "3",
   101	                                "--domain", kbc.Domain,
   102	                                "--namespaced",
   103	                        )
   104	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   105	
   106	                        By("verifying PROJECT file has namespaced: true")
   107	                        projectFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "PROJECT")
   108	                        content, err := os.ReadFile(projectFile)
   109	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   110	                        projectContent := string(content)
   111	                        // Check root level has namespaced: true
   112	                        beforeResources := strings.Split(projectContent, "resources:")[0]
   113	                        Expect(beforeResources).To(ContainSubstring("namespaced: true"))
   114	
   115	                        By("verifying RBAC is namespace-scoped")
   116	                        roleFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "rbac", "role.yaml")
   117	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(roleFile)
   118	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   119	                        roleContent := string(content)
   120	                        Expect(roleContent).To(ContainSubstring("kind: Role"))
   121	                        Expect(roleContent).NotTo(ContainSubstring("kind: ClusterRole"))
   122	
   123	                        roleBindingFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "rbac", "role_binding.yaml")
   124	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(roleBindingFile)
   125	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   126	                        bindingContent := string(content)
   127	                        Expect(bindingContent).To(ContainSubstring("kind: RoleBinding"))
   128	                        Expect(bindingContent).NotTo(ContainSubstring("kind: ClusterRoleBinding"))
   129	
   130	                        By("verifying manager.yaml has WATCH_NAMESPACE environment variable")
   131	                        managerFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "manager", "manager.yaml")
   132	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(managerFile)
   133	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   134	                        managerContent := string(content)
   135	                        Expect(managerContent).To(ContainSubstring("- name: WATCH_NAMESPACE"))
   136	                        Expect(managerContent).To(ContainSubstring("fieldRef:"))
   137	                        Expect(managerContent).To(ContainSubstring("fieldPath: metadata.namespace"))
   138	
   139	                        By("verifying cmd/main.go has getWatchNamespace helper function")
   140	                        mainFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "cmd", "main.go")
   141	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(mainFile)
   142	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   143	                        mainContent := string(content)
   144	                        Expect(mainContent).To(ContainSubstring("func getWatchNamespace()"))
   145	                        Expect(mainContent).To(ContainSubstring("WATCH_NAMESPACE"))
   146	
   147	                        By("verifying cmd/main.go has setupCacheNamespaces helper function")
   148	                        Expect(mainContent).To(ContainSubstring("func setupCacheNamespaces"))
   149	                        Expect(mainContent).To(ContainSubstring("DefaultNamespaces"))
   150	                        Expect(mainContent).To(ContainSubstring("cache.Config"))
   151	
   152	                        By("verifying cmd/main.go uses helper functions in main()")
   153	                        Expect(mainContent).To(ContainSubstring("watchNamespace, err := getWatchNamespace()"))
   154	                        Expect(mainContent).To(ContainSubstring("mgrOptions.Cache = setupCacheNamespaces(watchNamespace)"))
   155	
   156	                        By("creating an API to verify controller scaffolding")
   157	                        err = kbc.CreateAPI(
   158	                                "--group", kbc.Group,
   159	                                "--version", kbc.Version,
   160	                                "--kind", kbc.Kind,
   161	                                "--resource",
   162	                                "--controller",
   163	                                "--make=false",
   164	                        )
   165	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   166	
   167	                        By("verifying controller has namespace parameter in RBAC markers")
   168	                        controllerFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "internal", "controller",
   169	                                strings.ToLower(kbc.Kind)+"_controller.go")
   170	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(controllerFile)
   171	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   172	                        controllerContent := string(content)
   173	
   174	                        expectedNamespace := "e2e-" + kbc.TestSuffix + "-system"
   175	                        Expect(controllerContent).To(ContainSubstring("namespace="+expectedNamespace),
   176	                                "Controller RBAC markers should include namespace parameter")
   177	
   178	                        By("verifying admin/editor/viewer roles are namespace-scoped")
   179	                        editorFile := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "rbac",
   180	                                strings.ToLower(kbc.Kind)+"_editor_role.yaml")
   181	                        content, err = os.ReadFile(editorFile)
   182	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   183	                        Expect(string(content)).To(ContainSubstring("kind: Role"))
   184	                        Expect(string(content)).NotTo(ContainSubstring("kind: ClusterRole"))
   185	                })
   186	        })
   187	})

    29	var _ = Describe("kubebuilder", func() {
    30	        Context("plugin go/v4", func() {
    31	                var kbc *utils.TestContext
    32

    34	                        var err error
    35	                        kbc, err = utils.NewTestContext(util.KubebuilderBinName, "GO111MODULE=on")

    37	                        Expect(kbc.Prepare()).To(Succeed())
    38	                })
    39	
    40	                AfterEach(func() {
    41	                        By("removing restricted namespace label")
    42	                        _ = kbc.RemoveNamespaceLabelToEnforceRestricted()
    43	
    44	                        By("undeploy the project")
    45	                        _ = kbc.Make("undeploy")
    46	
    47	                        By("uninstalling the project")
    48	                        _ = kbc.Make("uninstall")
    49	
    50	                        By("removing controller image and working dir")
    51	                        kbc.Destroy()
    52	                })
    53	
    54	                It("should generate a runnable project", func() {
    55	                        helpers.GenerateV4(kbc)
    56	                        helpers.Run(kbc, helpers.RunOptions{
    57	                                HasWebhook:         true,
    58	                                HasMetrics:         true,
    59	                                HasNetworkPolicies: false,
    60	                                InstallMethod:      helpers.InstallMethodKustomize,
    61	                        })
    62	                })
    63	
    64	                It("should generate a runnable project with the Installer", func() {
    65	                        helpers.GenerateV4(kbc)

    67	                                HasWebhook:         true,
    68	                                HasMetrics:         true,
    69	                                HasNetworkPolicies: false,

    71	                        })
    72	                })

    78	                                HasMetrics:         false,
    79	                                HasNetworkPolicies: false,
    80	                                InstallMethod:      helpers.InstallMethodKustomize,
    81	                        })
    82	                })
    83

    87	                                HasWebhook:         false,
    88	                                HasMetrics:         true,

    92	                })

    99	                                HasNetworkPolicies: true,
   100	                                InstallMethod:      helpers.InstallMethodKustomize,
   101	                        })
   102	                })

    21	        "os"
    22	        "path/filepath"
    23	        "strings"
    24	
    25	        . "github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2" //nolint:staticcheck
    26	        . "github.com/onsi/gomega"    //nolint:staticcheck
    27	
    28	        pluginutil "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/plugin/util"
    29	        "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/test/e2e/utils"
    30	)

    32	// GenerateV4 implements a go/v4 plugin project defined by a TestContext.
    33	func GenerateV4(kbc *utils.TestContext) {
    34	        initingTheProject(kbc)
    35	        creatingAPI(kbc)
    36	
    37	        By("scaffolding mutating and validating webhooks")
    38	        err := kbc.CreateWebhook(
    39	                "--group", kbc.Group,
    40	                "--version", kbc.Version,
    41	                "--kind", kbc.Kind,
    42	                "--defaulting",
    43	                "--programmatic-validation",
    44	                "--make=false",
    45	        )
    46	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to scaffolding mutating webhook")
    47	
    48	        By("implementing the mutating and validating webhooks")
    49	        webhookFilePath := filepath.Join(
    50	                kbc.Dir, "internal/webhook", kbc.Version,
    51	                fmt.Sprintf("%s_webhook.go", strings.ToLower(kbc.Kind)))
    52	        err = utils.ImplementWebhooks(webhookFilePath, strings.ToLower(kbc.Kind))
    53	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to implement webhooks")
    54	
    55	        scaffoldConversionWebhook(kbc)
    56	
    57	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.UncommentCode(
    58	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "default", "kustomization.yaml"),
    59	                "#- ../prometheus", "#")).To(Succeed())
    60	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.UncommentCode(
    61	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "prometheus", "kustomization.yaml"),
    62	                monitorTLSPatch, "#")).To(Succeed())
    63	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.UncommentCode(
    64	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "default", "kustomization.yaml"),
    65	                metricsCertPatch, "#")).To(Succeed())
    66	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.UncommentCode(
    67	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "default", "kustomization.yaml"),
    68	                metricsCertReplaces, "#")).To(Succeed())
    69	}
    70	
    71	// GenerateV4WithoutMetrics implements a go/v4 plugin project defined by a TestContext.
    72	func GenerateV4WithoutMetrics(kbc *utils.TestContext) {
    73	        initingTheProject(kbc)
    74	        creatingAPI(kbc)
    75	
    76	        By("scaffolding mutating and validating webhooks")
    77	        err := kbc.CreateWebhook(
    78	                "--group", kbc.Group,
    79	                "--version", kbc.Version,
    80	                "--kind", kbc.Kind,
    81	                "--defaulting",
    82	                "--programmatic-validation",
    83	                "--make=false",
    84	        )
    85	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to scaffolding mutating webhook")
    86	
    87	        By("implementing the mutating and validating webhooks")
    88	        webhookFilePath := filepath.Join(
    89	                kbc.Dir, "internal/webhook", kbc.Version,
    90	                fmt.Sprintf("%s_webhook.go", strings.ToLower(kbc.Kind)))
    91	        err = utils.ImplementWebhooks(webhookFilePath, strings.ToLower(kbc.Kind))
    92	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to implement webhooks")
    93	
    94	        scaffoldConversionWebhook(kbc)
    95	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.UncommentCode(
    96	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "default", "kustomization.yaml"),
    97	                "#- ../prometheus", "#")).To(Succeed())
    98	        // Disable metrics
    99	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.CommentCode(
   100	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "default", "kustomization.yaml"),
   101	                "- metrics_service.yaml", "#")).To(Succeed())
   102	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.CommentCode(
   103	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "default", "kustomization.yaml"),
   104	                metricsTarget, "#")).To(Succeed())
   105	}

   180	// GenerateV4WithCustomWebhookPath tests webhooks with custom paths
   181	func GenerateV4WithCustomWebhookPath(kbc *utils.TestContext) {
   182	        initingTheProject(kbc)
   183	        creatingAPI(kbc)
   184	
   185	        By("scaffolding both defaulting and validation webhooks with different custom paths")
   186	        err := kbc.CreateWebhook(
   187	                "--group", kbc.Group,
   188	                "--version", kbc.Version,
   189	                "--kind", kbc.Kind,
   190	                "--defaulting",
   191	                "--programmatic-validation",
   192	                "--defaulting-path=/custom-mutate-path",
   193	                "--validation-path=/custom-validate-path",
   194	                "--make=false",
   195	        )
   196	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to scaffold webhooks with custom paths")
   197	
   198	        By("verifying custom webhook paths in generated webhook file")
   199	        webhookFilePath := filepath.Join(
   200	                kbc.Dir, "internal/webhook", kbc.Version,
   201	                fmt.Sprintf("%s_webhook.go", strings.ToLower(kbc.Kind)))
   202	
   203	        // Read the webhook file and check if both custom paths are present
   204	        content, err := os.ReadFile(webhookFilePath)
   205	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to read webhook file")
   206	        Expect(string(content)).To(ContainSubstring("path=/custom-mutate-path"),
   207	                "Webhook file should contain custom defaulting path")
   208	        Expect(string(content)).To(ContainSubstring("path=/custom-validate-path"),
   209	                "Webhook file should contain custom validation path")
   210	
   211	        By("implementing the webhooks")
   212	        err = utils.ImplementWebhooks(webhookFilePath, strings.ToLower(kbc.Kind))
   213	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to implement webhook")
   214	
   215	        scaffoldConversionWebhook(kbc)
   216	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.UncommentCode(
   217	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "config", "default", "kustomization.yaml"),
   218	                "#- ../prometheus", "#")).To(Succeed())
   219	
   220	        By("verifying that --defaulting-path requires --defaulting flag")
   221	        err = kbc.CreateWebhook(
   222	                "--group", kbc.Group,
   223	                "--version", kbc.Version,
   224	                "--kind", "InvalidTest",
   225	                "--defaulting-path=/invalid-path",
   226	                "--make=false",
   227	        )
   228	        Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred(), "Should fail when --defaulting-path is used without --defaulting")
   229	        Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring("--defaulting-path can only be used with --defaulting"))
   230	
   231	        By("verifying that --validation-path requires --programmatic-validation flag")
   232	        err = kbc.CreateWebhook(
   233	                "--group", kbc.Group,
   234	                "--version", kbc.Version,
   235	                "--kind", "InvalidTest",
   236	                "--validation-path=/invalid-path",
   237	                "--make=false",
   238	        )
   239	        Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred(), "Should fail when --validation-path is used without --programmatic-validation")
   240	        Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring("--validation-path can only be used with --programmatic-validation"))
   241	}

   291	// scaffoldConversionWebhook sets up conversion webhooks for testing the ConversionTest API
   292	func scaffoldConversionWebhook(kbc *utils.TestContext) {
   293	        By("scaffolding conversion webhooks for testing ConversionTest v1 to v2 conversion")
   294	
   295	        // Create API for v1 (hub) with conversion enabled
   296	        err := kbc.CreateAPI(
   297	                "--group", kbc.Group,
   298	                "--version", "v1",
   299	                "--kind", "ConversionTest",
   300	                "--controller=true",
   301	                "--resource=true",
   302	                "--make=false",
   303	        )
   304	        ExpectWithOffset(1, err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "failed to create v1 API for conversion testing")
   305	
   306	        // Create API for v2 (spoke) without a controller
   307	        err = kbc.CreateAPI(
   308	                "--group", kbc.Group,
   309	                "--version", "v2",
   310	                "--kind", "ConversionTest",
   311	                "--controller=false",
   312	                "--resource=true",
   313	                "--make=false",
   314	        )
   315	        ExpectWithOffset(1, err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "failed to create v2 API for conversion testing")
   316	
   317	        // Create the conversion webhook for v1
   318	        By("setting up the conversion webhook for v1")
   319	        err = kbc.CreateWebhook(
   320	                "--group", kbc.Group,
   321	                "--version", "v1",
   322	                "--kind", "ConversionTest",
   323	                "--conversion",
   324	                "--spoke", "v2",
   325	                "--make=false",
   326	        )
   327	        ExpectWithOffset(1, err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "failed to create conversion webhook for v1")
   328	
   329	        // Insert Size field in v1
   330	        By("implementing the size spec in v1")
   331	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.InsertCode(
   332	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "api", "v1", "conversiontest_types.go"),
   333	                "Foo *string `json:\"foo,omitempty\"`",
   334	                "\n\tSize int `json:\"size,omitempty\"` // Number of desired instances",
   335	        )).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "failed to add size spec to conversiontest_types v1")
   336	
   337	        // Insert Replicas field in v2
   338	        By("implementing the replicas spec in v2")
   339	        ExpectWithOffset(1, pluginutil.InsertCode(
   340	                filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "api", "v2", "conversiontest_types.go"),
   341	                "Foo *string `json:\"foo,omitempty\"`",
   342	                "\n\tReplicas int `json:\"replicas,omitempty\"` // Number of replicas",
   343	        )).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "failed to add replicas spec to conversiontest_conversion.go v2")
   344	
   345	        err = pluginutil.ReplaceInFile(filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "api/v2/conversiontest_conversion.go"),
   346	                "// dst.Spec.Size = src.Spec.Replicas",
   347	                "dst.Spec.Size = src.Spec.Replicas")
   348	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "failed to implement conversion logic from v1 to v2")
   349	
   350	        err = pluginutil.ReplaceInFile(filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "api/v2/conversiontest_conversion.go"),
   351	                "// dst.Spec.Replicas = src.Spec.Size",
   352	                "dst.Spec.Replicas = src.Spec.Size")
   353	        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "failed to implement conversion logic from v2 to v1")
   354	}

Looking at the PR description again:
- "When kubebuilder reads `boilerplate.go.txt` from disk before scaffolding new files (e.g. `kubebuilder create api`), it replaces `YEAR` with the current year"

The simplest approach is to add a utility function that does the replacement where the boilerplate is read. Let me add it to the `boilerplate.go` file itself since it's about boilerplate processing:







🎬 ACTION
str_replace_editor str_replace /testbed/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/hack/boilerplate.go   --old_str 'import (
        "path/filepath"

        "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/machinery"
)

// DefaultBoilerplatePath is the default path to the boilerplate file
var DefaultBoilerplatePath = filepath.Join("hack", "boilerplate.go.txt")' --new_str 'import (
        "fmt"
        "path/filepath"
        "strings"
        "time"

        "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/machinery"
)

// DefaultBoilerplatePath is the default path to the boilerplate file
var DefaultBoilerplatePath = filepath.Join("hack", "boilerplate.go.txt")

// ReplaceBoilerplateYear replaces the YEAR placeholder in the boilerplate with the current year.
// This matches the behavior of both gengo and controller-gen, which substitute YEAR
// with the current year at code-generation time.
func ReplaceBoilerplateYear(boilerplate string) string {
        return strings.ReplaceAll(boilerplate, "YEAR", fmt.Sprintf("%v", time.Now().Year()))
}'

					"error", err)

			} else {

			}

		boilerplateStr := hack.ReplaceBoilerplateYear(string(boilerplate))
		// Initialize the machinery.Scaffold that will write the files to disk
		scaffold = machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
			machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
			machinery.WithBoilerplate(boilerplateStr),

str_replace_editor str_replace /testbed/pkg/plugins/golang/v4/scaffolds/internal/templates/makefile.go   --old_str '//nolint:lll
const makefileTemplate = `# Image URL to use all building/pushing image targets
IMG ?= {{ .Image }}

# Get the currently used golang install path (in GOPATH/bin, unless GOBIN is set)
ifeq (,$(shell go env GOBIN))
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOPATH)/bin
else
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOBIN)
endif

# CONTAINER_TOOL defines the container tool to be used for building images.
# Be aware that the target commands are only tested with Docker which is
# scaffolded by default. However, you might want to replace it to use other
# tools. (i.e. podman)
CONTAINER_TOOL ?= docker

# Setting SHELL to bash allows bash commands to be executed by recipes.
# Options are set to exit when a recipe line exits non-zero or a piped command fails.
SHELL = /usr/bin/env bash -o pipefail
.SHELLFLAGS = -ec

.PHONY: all
all: build

##@ General

# The help target prints out all targets with their descriptions organized
# beneath their categories. The categories are represented by '"'"'##@'"'"' and the
# target descriptions by '"'"'##'"'"'. The awk command is responsible for reading the
# entire set of makefiles included in this invocation, looking for lines of the
# file as xyz: ## something, and then pretty-format the target and help. Then,
# if there'"'"'s a line with ##@ something, that gets pretty-printed as a category.
# More info on the usage of ANSI control characters for terminal formatting:
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#SGR_parameters
# More info on the awk command:
# http://linuxcommand.org/lc3_adv_awk.php

.PHONY: help
help: ## Display this help.
        @awk '"'"'BEGIN {FS = ":.*##"; printf "\nUsage:\n  make \033[36m<target>\033[0m\n"} /^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+:.*?##/ { printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2 } /^##@/ { printf "\n\033[1m%s\033[0m\n", substr($$0, 5) } '"'"' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)

##@ Development

.PHONY: manifests
manifests: controller-gen ## Generate WebhookConfiguration, ClusterRole and CustomResourceDefinition objects.
        "$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" rbac:roleName=manager-role crd webhook paths="./..." output:crd:artifacts:config=config/crd/bases

.PHONY: generate
generate: controller-gen ## Generate code containing DeepCopy, DeepCopyInto, and DeepCopyObject method implementations.
        {{ if .BoilerplatePath -}}
        "$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object:headerFile={{printf "%q" .BoilerplatePath}} paths="./..."
        {{- else -}}
        "$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object paths="./..."
        {{- end }}

.PHONY: fmt
fmt: ## Run go fmt against code.
        go fmt ./...

.PHONY: vet
vet: ## Run go vet against code.
        go vet ./...

.PHONY: test
test: manifests generate fmt vet setup-envtest ## Run tests.
        KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(shell "$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path)" go test $$(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e) -coverprofile cover.out

# TODO(user): To use a different vendor for e2e tests, modify the setup under '"'"'tests/e2e'"'"'.
# The default setup assumes Kind is pre-installed and builds/loads the Manager Docker image locally.
# CertManager is installed by default; skip with:
# - CERT_MANAGER_INSTALL_SKIP=true
KIND_CLUSTER ?= {{ .ProjectName }}-test-e2e

.PHONY: setup-test-e2e
setup-test-e2e: ## Set up a Kind cluster for e2e tests if it does not exist
        @command -v $(KIND) >/dev/null 2>&1 || { \
                echo "Kind is not installed. Please install Kind manually."; \
                exit 1; \
        }
        @case "$$($(KIND) get clusters)" in \
                *"$(KIND_CLUSTER)"*) \
                        echo "Kind cluster '"'"'$(KIND_CLUSTER)'"'"' already exists. Skipping creation." ;; \
                *) \
                        echo "Creating Kind cluster '"'"'$(KIND_CLUSTER)'"'"'..."; \
                        $(KIND) create cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER) ;; \
        esac

.PHONY: test-e2e
test-e2e: setup-test-e2e manifests generate fmt vet ## Run the e2e tests. Expected an isolated environment using Kind.
        KIND=$(KIND) KIND_CLUSTER=$(KIND_CLUSTER) go test -tags=e2e ./test/e2e/ -v -ginkgo.v
        $(MAKE) cleanup-test-e2e

.PHONY: cleanup-test-e2e
cleanup-test-e2e: ## Tear down the Kind cluster used for e2e tests
        @$(KIND) delete cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER)

.PHONY: lint
lint: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter
        "$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run

.PHONY: lint-fix
lint-fix: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter and perform fixes
        "$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run --fix

.PHONY: lint-config
lint-config: golangci-lint ## Verify golangci-lint linter configuration
        "$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" config verify

##@ Build

.PHONY: build
build: manifests generate fmt vet ## Build manager binary.
        go build -o bin/manager cmd/main.go

.PHONY: run
run: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run a controller from your host.
        go run ./cmd/main.go

# If you wish to build the manager image targeting other platforms you can use the --platform flag.
# (i.e. docker build --platform linux/arm64). However, you must enable docker buildKit for it.
# More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
.PHONY: docker-build
docker-build: ## Build docker image with the manager.
        $(CONTAINER_TOOL) build -t ${IMG} .

.PHONY: docker-push
docker-push: ## Push docker image with the manager.
        $(CONTAINER_TOOL) push ${IMG}

# PLATFORMS defines the target platforms for the manager image be built to provide support to multiple
# architectures. (i.e. make docker-buildx IMG=myregistry/mypoperator:0.0.1). To use this option you need to:
# - be able to use docker buildx. More info: https://docs.docker.com/build/buildx/
# - have enabled BuildKit. More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
# - be able to push the image to your registry (i.e. if you do not set a valid value via IMG=<myregistry/image:<tag>> then the export will fail)
# To adequately provide solutions that are compatible with multiple platforms, you should consider using this option.
PLATFORMS ?= linux/arm64,linux/amd64,linux/s390x,linux/ppc64le
.PHONY: docker-buildx
docker-buildx: ## Build and push docker image for the manager for cross-platform support
        # copy existing Dockerfile and insert --platform=${BUILDPLATFORM} into Dockerfile.cross, and preserve the original Dockerfile
        sed -e '"'"'1 s/\(^FROM\)/FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/; t'"'"' -e '"'"' 1,// s//FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/'"'"' Dockerfile > Dockerfile.cross
        - $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx create --name {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
        $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx use {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
        - $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --push --platform=$(PLATFORMS) --tag ${IMG} -f Dockerfile.cross .
        - $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx rm {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
        rm Dockerfile.cross

.PHONY: build-installer
build-installer: manifests generate kustomize ## Generate a consolidated YAML with CRDs and deployment.
        mkdir -p dist
        cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
        "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default > dist/install.yaml

##@ Deployment

ifndef ignore-not-found
  ignore-not-found = false
endif

.PHONY: install
install: manifests kustomize ## Install CRDs into the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
        @out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
        if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -; else echo "No CRDs to install; skipping."; fi

.PHONY: uninstall
uninstall: manifests kustomize ## Uninstall CRDs from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
        @out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
        if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -; else echo "No CRDs to delete; skipping."; fi

.PHONY: deploy
deploy: manifests kustomize ## Deploy controller to the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
        cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
        "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -

.PHONY: undeploy
undeploy: kustomize ## Undeploy controller from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
        "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -

##@ Dependencies

## Location to install dependencies to
LOCALBIN ?= $(shell pwd)/bin
$(LOCALBIN):
        mkdir -p "$(LOCALBIN)"

## Tool Binaries
KUBECTL ?= kubectl
KIND ?= kind
KUSTOMIZE ?= $(LOCALBIN)/kustomize
CONTROLLER_GEN ?= $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen
ENVTEST ?= $(LOCALBIN)/setup-envtest
GOLANGCI_LINT = $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint

## Tool Versions
KUSTOMIZE_VERSION ?= {{ .KustomizeVersion }}
CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= {{ .ControllerToolsVersion }}

#ENVTEST_VERSION is the version of controller-runtime release branch to fetch the envtest setup script (i.e. release-0.20)
ENVTEST_VERSION ?= $(shell v='"'"'$(call gomodver,sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime)'"'"'; \
  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_VERSION manually (controller-runtime replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
  printf '"'"'%s\n'"'"' "$$v" | sed -E '"'"'s/^v?([0-9]+)\.([0-9]+).*/release-\1.\2/'"'"')

#ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION is the version of Kubernetes to use for setting up ENVTEST binaries (i.e. 1.31)
ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION ?= $(shell v='"'"'$(call gomodver,k8s.io/api)'"'"'; \
  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION manually (k8s.io/api replace has no tag)" >&2; exit 1; }; \
  printf '"'"'%s\n'"'"' "$$v" | sed -E '"'"'s/^v?[0-9]+\.([0-9]+).*/1.\1/'"'"')

GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION ?= {{ .GolangciLintVersion }}
.PHONY: kustomize
kustomize: $(KUSTOMIZE) ## Download kustomize locally if necessary.
$(KUSTOMIZE): $(LOCALBIN)
        $(call go-install-tool,$(KUSTOMIZE),sigs.k8s.io/kustomize/kustomize/v5,$(KUSTOMIZE_VERSION))

.PHONY: controller-gen
controller-gen: $(CONTROLLER_GEN) ## Download controller-gen locally if necessary.
$(CONTROLLER_GEN): $(LOCALBIN)
        $(call go-install-tool,$(CONTROLLER_GEN),sigs.k8s.io/controller-tools/cmd/controller-gen,$(CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION))

.PHONY: setup-envtest
setup-envtest: envtest ## Download the binaries required for ENVTEST in the local bin directory.
        @echo "Setting up envtest binaries for Kubernetes version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)..."
        @"$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path || { \
                echo "Error: Failed to set up envtest binaries for version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)."; \
                exit 1; \
        }

.PHONY: envtest
envtest: $(ENVTEST) ## Download setup-envtest locally if necessary.
$(ENVTEST): $(LOCALBIN)
        $(call go-install-tool,$(ENVTEST),sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/tools/setup-envtest,$(ENVTEST_VERSION))

.PHONY: golangci-lint
golangci-lint: $(GOLANGCI_LINT) ## Download golangci-lint locally if necessary.
$(GOLANGCI_LINT): $(LOCALBIN)
        $(call go-install-tool,$(GOLANGCI_LINT),github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint,$(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION))
        @test -f .custom-gcl.yml && { \
                echo "Building custom golangci-lint with plugins..." && \
                $(GOLANGCI_LINT) custom --destination $(LOCALBIN) --name golangci-lint-custom && \
                mv -f $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint-custom $(GOLANGCI_LINT); \
        } || true

# go-install-tool will '"'"'go install'"'"' any package with custom target and name of binary, if it doesn'"'"'t exist
# $1 - target path with name of binary
# $2 - package url which can be installed
# $3 - specific version of package
define go-install-tool
@[ -f "$(1)-$(3)" ] && [ "$$(readlink -- "$(1)" 2>/dev/null)" = "$(1)-$(3)" ] || { \
set -e; \
package=$(2)@$(3) ;\
echo "Downloading $${package}" ;\
rm -f "$(1)" ;\
GOBIN="$(LOCALBIN)" go install $${package} ;\
mv "$(LOCALBIN)/$$(basename "$(1)")" "$(1)-$(3)" ;\
} ;\
ln -sf "$$(realpath "$(1)-$(3)")" "$(1)"
endef

define gomodver
$(shell go list -m -f '"'"'{{"{{"}}if .Replace{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}.Replace.Version{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}else{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}.Version{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}end{{"}}"}}'"'"' $(1) 2>/dev/null)
endef
`' --new_str '//nolint:lll
const makefileTemplate = `# Image URL to use all building/pushing image targets
IMG ?= {{ .Image }}

# Get the currently used golang install path (in GOPATH/bin, unless GOBIN is set)
ifeq (,$(shell go env GOBIN))
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOPATH)/bin
else
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOBIN)
endif

# CONTAINER_TOOL defines the container tool to be used for building images.
# Be aware that the target commands are only tested with Docker which is
# scaffolded by default. However, you might want to replace it to use other
# tools. (i.e. podman)
CONTAINER_TOOL ?= docker

# Setting SHELL to bash allows bash commands to be executed by recipes.
# Options are set to exit when a recipe line exits non-zero or a piped command fails.
SHELL = /usr/bin/env bash -o pipefail
.SHELLFLAGS = -ec

.PHONY: all
all: build

##@ General

# The help target prints out all targets with their descriptions organized
# beneath their categories. The categories are represented by '"'"'##@'"'"' and the
# target descriptions by '"'"'##'"'"'. The awk command is responsible for reading the
# entire set of makefiles included in this invocation, looking for lines of the
# file as xyz: ## something, and then pretty-format the target and help. Then,
# if there'"'"'s a line with ##@ something, that gets pretty-printed as a category.
# More info on the usage of ANSI control characters for terminal formatting:
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#SGR_parameters
# More info on the awk command:
# http://linuxcommand.org/lc3_adv_awk.php

.PHONY: help
help: ## Display this help.
        @awk '"'"'BEGIN {FS = ":.*##"; printf "\nUsage:\n  make \033[36m<target>\033[0m\n"} /^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+:.*?##/ { printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2 } /^##@/ { printf "\n\033[1m%s\033[0m\n", substr($$0, 5) } '"'"' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)

const makefileTemplate = `# Image URL to use all building/pushing image targets
IMG ?= {{ .Image }}

# Get the currently used golang install path (in GOPATH/bin, unless GOBIN is set)
ifeq (,$(shell go env GOBIN))
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOPATH)/bin
else
GOBIN=$(shell go env GOBIN)
endif

# CONTAINER_TOOL defines the container tool to be used for building images.
# Be aware that the target commands are only tested with Docker which is
# scaffolded by default. However, you might want to replace it to use other
# tools. (i.e. podman)
CONTAINER_TOOL ?= docker

# Setting SHELL to bash allows bash commands to be executed by recipes.
# Options are set to exit when a recipe line exits non-zero or a piped command fails.
SHELL = /usr/bin/env bash -o pipefail
.SHELLFLAGS = -ec

.PHONY: all
all: build

##@ General

# The help target prints out all targets with their descriptions organized
# beneath their categories. The categories are represented by '##@' and the
# target descriptions by '##'. The awk command is responsible for reading the
# entire set of makefiles included in this invocation, looking for lines of the
# file as xyz: ## something, and then pretty-format the target and help. Then,
# if there's a line with ##@ something, that gets pretty-printed as a category.
# More info on the usage of ANSI control characters for terminal formatting:
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#SGR_parameters
# More info on the awk command:
# http://linuxcommand.org/lc3_adv_awk.php

.PHONY: help
help: ## Display this help.
        @awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*##"; printf "\nUsage:\n  make \033[36m<target>\033[0m\n"} /^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+:.*?##/ { printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2 } /^##@/ { printf "\n\033[1m%s\033[0m\n", substr($$0, 5) } ' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)

##@ Development

.PHONY: manifests
manifests: controller-gen ## Generate WebhookConfiguration, ClusterRole and CustomResourceDefinition objects.
        "$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" rbac:roleName=manager-role crd webhook paths="./..." output:crd:artifacts:config=config/crd/bases

.PHONY: generate
generate: controller-gen ## Generate code containing DeepCopy, DeepCopyInto, and DeepCopyObject method implementations.
        {{ if .BoilerplatePath -}}
        "$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object:headerFile={{printf "%q" .BoilerplatePath}} paths="./..."
        {{- else -}}
        "$(CONTROLLER_GEN)" object paths="./..."
        {{- end }}

.PHONY: fmt
fmt: ## Run go fmt against code.
        go fmt ./...

.PHONY: vet
vet: ## Run go vet against code.
        go vet ./...

.PHONY: test
test: manifests generate fmt vet setup-envtest ## Run tests.
        KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(shell "$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path)" go test $$(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e) -coverprofile cover.out

# TODO(user): To use a different vendor for e2e tests, modify the setup under 'tests/e2e'.
# The default setup assumes Kind is pre-installed and builds/loads the Manager Docker image locally.
# CertManager is installed by default; skip with:
# - CERT_MANAGER_INSTALL_SKIP=true
KIND_CLUSTER ?= {{ .ProjectName }}-test-e2e

.PHONY: setup-test-e2e
setup-test-e2e: ## Set up a Kind cluster for e2e tests if it does not exist
        @command -v $(KIND) >/dev/null 2>&1 || { \
                echo "Kind is not installed. Please install Kind manually."; \
 (exit 1); \
        }
        @case "$$($(KIND) get clusters)" in \
                *"$(KIND_CLUSTER)"*) \
                        echo "Kind cluster '$(KIND_CLUSTER)' already exists. Skipping creation." ;; \
                *) \
                        echo "Creating Kind cluster '$(KIND_CLUSTER)'..."; \
                        $(KIND) create cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER) ;; \
        esac

.PHONY: test-e2e
test-e2e: setup-test-e2e manifests generate fmt vet ## Run the e2e tests. Expected an isolated environment using Kind.
        KIND=$(KIND) KIND_CLUSTER=$(KIND_CLUSTER) go test -tags=e2e ./test/e2e/ -v -ginkgo.v
        $(MAKE) cleanup-test-e2e

.PHONY: cleanup-test-e2e
cleanup-test-e2e: ## Tear down the Kind cluster used for e2e tests
        @$(KIND) delete cluster --name $(KIND_CLUSTER)

.PHONY: lint
lint: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter
        "$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run

.PHONY: lint-fix
lint-fix: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint linter and perform fixes
        "$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" run --fix

.PHONY: lint-config
lint-config: golangci-lint ## Verify golangci-lint linter configuration
        "$(GOLANGCI_LINT)" config verify

##@ Build

.PHONY: build
build: manifests generate fmt vet ## Build manager binary.
        go build -o bin/manager cmd/main.go

.PHONY: run
run: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run a controller from your host.
        go run ./cmd/main.go

# If you wish to build the manager image targeting other platforms you can use the --platform flag.
# (i.e. docker build --platform linux/arm64). However, you must enable docker buildKit for it.
# More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
.PHONY: docker-build
docker-build: ## Build docker image with the manager.
        $(CONTAINER_TOOL) build -t ${IMG} .

.PHONY: docker-push
docker-push: ## Push docker image with the manager.
        $(CONTAINER_TOOL) push ${IMG}

# PLATFORMS defines the target platforms for the manager image be built to provide support to multiple
# architectures. (i.e. make docker-buildx IMG=myregistry/mypoperator:0.0.1). To use this option you need to:
# - be able to use docker buildx. More info: https://docs.docker.com/build/buildx/
# - have enabled BuildKit. More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
# - be able to push the image to your registry (i.e. if you do not set a valid value via IMG=<myregistry/image:<tag>> then the export will fail)
# To adequately provide solutions that are compatible with multiple platforms, you should consider using this option.
PLATFORMS ?= linux/arm64,linux/amd64,linux/s390x,linux/ppc64le
.PHONY: docker-buildx
docker-buildx: ## Build and push docker image for the manager for cross-platform support
        # copy existing Dockerfile and insert --platform=${BUILDPLATFORM} into Dockerfile.cross, and preserve the original Dockerfile
        sed -e '1 s/\(^FROM\)/FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/; t' -e ' 1,// s//FROM --platform=\$$\{BUILDPLATFORM\}/' Dockerfile > Dockerfile.cross
        - $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx create --name {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
        $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx use {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
        - $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --push --platform=$(PLATFORMS) --tag ${IMG} -f Dockerfile.cross .
        - $(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx rm {{ .ProjectName }}-builder
        rm Dockerfile.cross

.PHONY: build-installer
build-installer: manifests generate kustomize ## Generate a consolidated YAML with CRDs and deployment.
        mkdir -p dist
        cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
        "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default > dist/install.yaml

##@ Deployment

ifndef ignore-not-found
  ignore-not-found = false
endif

.PHONY: install
install: manifests kustomize ## Install CRDs into the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
        @out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
        if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -; else echo "No CRDs to install; skipping."; fi

.PHONY: uninstall
uninstall: manifests kustomize ## Uninstall CRDs from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
        @out="$$( "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/crd 2>/dev/null || true )"; \
        if [ -n "$$out" ]; then echo "$$out" | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -; else echo "No CRDs to delete; skipping."; fi

.PHONY: deploy
deploy: manifests kustomize ## Deploy controller to the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.
        cd config/manager && "$(KUSTOMIZE)" edit set image controller=${IMG}
        "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" apply -f -

.PHONY: undeploy
undeploy: kustomize ## Undeploy controller from the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config. Call with ignore-not-found=true to ignore resource not found errors during deletion.
        "$(KUSTOMIZE)" build config/default | "$(KUBECTL)" delete --ignore-not-found=$(ignore-not-found) -f -

##@ Dependencies

LOCALBIN ?= $(shell pwd)/bin
$(LOCALBIN):
        mkdir -p "$(LOCALBIN)"

KIND ?= kind

CONTROLLER_GEN ?= $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen
ENVTEST ?= $(LOCALBIN)/setup-envtest

## Tool Versions
KUSTOMIZE_VERSION ?= {{ .KustomizeVersion }}
CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= {{ .ControllerToolsVersion }}

#ENVTEST_VERSION is the version of controller-runtime release branch to fetch the envtest setup script (i.e. release-0.20)
ENVTEST_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime)'; \
  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_VERSION manually (controller-runtime replace has no tag)" >&2; (exit 1); }; \
  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?([0-9]+)\.([0-9]+).*/release-\1.\2/')

#ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION is the version of Kubernetes to use for setting up ENVTEST binaries (i.e. 1.31)
ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION ?= $(shell v='$(call gomodver,k8s.io/api)'; \
  [ -n "$$v" ] || { echo "Set ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION manually (k8s.io/api replace has no tag)" >&2; (exit 1); }; \
  printf '%s\n' "$$v" | sed -E 's/^v?[0-9]+\.([0-9]+).*/1.\1/')

GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION ?= {{ .GolangciLintVersion }}

        @echo "Setting up envtest binaries for Kubernetes version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)..."
        @"$(ENVTEST)" use $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION) --bin-dir "$(LOCALBIN)" -p path || { \
                echo "Error: Failed to set up envtest binaries for version $(ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION)."; \

        }

        $(call go-install-tool,$(ENVTEST),sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/tools/setup-envtest,$(ENVTEST_VERSION))

.PHONY: golangci-lint

$(GOLANGCI_LINT): $(LOCALBIN)
        $(call go-install-tool,$(GOLANGCI_LINT),github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint,$(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION))
        @test -f .custom-gcl.yml && { \
                echo "Building custom golangci-lint with plugins..." && \
                $(GOLANGCI_LINT) custom --destination $(LOCALBIN) --name golangci-lint-custom && \
                mv -f $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint-custom $(GOLANGCI_LINT); \
        } || true

# go-install-tool will 'go install' any package with custom target and name of binary, if it doesn't exist
# $1 - target path with name of binary
# $2 - package url which can be installed
# $3 - specific version of package
define go-install-tool
@[ -f "$(1)-$(3)" ] && [ "$$(readlink -- "$(1)" 2>/dev/null)" = "$(1)-$(3)" ] || { \
set -e; \
package=$(2)@$(3) ;\
echo "Downloading $${package}" ;\
rm -f "$(1)" ;\
GOBIN="$(LOCALBIN)" go install $${package} ;\
mv "$(LOCALBIN)/$$(basename "$(1)")" "$(1)-$(3)" ;\
} ;\
ln -sf "$$(realpath "$(1)-$(3)")" "$(1)"
endef

define gomodver
$(shell go list -m -f '{{"{{"}}if .Replace{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}.Replace.Version{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}else{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}.Version{{"}}"}}{{"{{"}}end{{"}}"}}' $(1) 2>/dev/null)
endef

    58	                                        Affinity: &corev1.Affinity{
    59	                                                NodeAffinity: &corev1.NodeAffinity{
    60	                                                        RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution: &corev1.NodeSelector{

    72	        testOperator := &webappv1.TestOperator{}
    73	        err := r.Get(ctx, req.NamespacedName, testOperator)
    74	        if err != nil {
    75	                if errors.IsNotFound(err) {
