    91	
    92	var _ = Describe("kubebuilder", func() {
    93	        Context("alpha update", func() {
    94	                var (
    95	                        pathBinFromVersion string
    96	                        kbc                *utils.TestContext
    97	                )
    98	
    99	                BeforeEach(func() {
   100	                        var err error
   101	                        By("setting up test context with binary build from source")
   102	                        kbc, err = utils.NewTestContext(util.KubebuilderBinName, "GO111MODULE=on")
   103	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   104	                        Expect(kbc.Prepare()).To(Succeed())
   105	
   106	                        pathBinFromVersion, err = downloadKubebuilderVersion(fromVersion)
   107	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   108	
   109	                        cmd := exec.Command(pathBinFromVersion, "init", "--domain", "example.com", "--repo",
   110	                                "github.com/example/test-operator")
   111	                        cmd.Dir = kbc.Dir
   112	                        output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
   113	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), fmt.Sprintf("init failed: %s", output))
   114	
   115	                        cmd = exec.Command(pathBinFromVersion, "create", "api", "--group", "webapp", "--version", "v1",
   116	                                "--kind", "TestOperator", "--resource", "--controller")
   117	                        cmd.Dir = kbc.Dir
   118	                        output, err = cmd.CombinedOutput()
   119	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), fmt.Sprintf("create api failed: %s", output))
   120	                        Expect(kbc.Make("generate", "manifests")).To(Succeed())
   121	
   122	                        updateAPI(kbc.Dir)
   123	                        updateController(kbc.Dir)
   124	                        initializeGitRepo(kbc.Dir)
   125	                })
   126	
   127	                AfterEach(func() {
   128	                        By("cleaning up test artifacts")
   129	                        _ = os.RemoveAll(filepath.Dir(pathBinFromVersion))
   130	                        _ = os.RemoveAll(kbc.Dir)
   131	                        kbc.Destroy()
   132	                })
   133	
   134	                It("should update project from v4.5.2 to v4.6.0 without conflicts", func() {
   135	                        By("running alpha update from v4.5.2 to v4.6.0")
   136	                        cmd := exec.Command(
   137	                                kbc.BinaryName, "alpha", "update",
   138	                                "--from-version", fromVersion,
   139	                                "--to-version", toVersion,
   140	                                "--from-branch", "main",
   141	                        )
   142	                        cmd.Dir = kbc.Dir
   143	                        out, err := kbc.Run(cmd)
   144	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), string(out))
   145	
   146	                        By("checking that custom code is preserved")
   147	                        validateCustomCodePreservation(kbc.Dir)
   148	
   149	                        By("checking that no conflict markers are present in the project files")
   150	                        Expect(hasConflictMarkers(kbc.Dir)).To(BeFalse())
   151	
   152	                        By("checking that go module is upgraded")
   153	                        validateCommonGoModule(kbc.Dir)
   154	
   155	                        By("checking that Makefile is updated")
   156	                        validateMakefileContent(kbc.Dir)
   157	
   158	                        By("checking temporary branches were cleaned up locally")
   159	                        outRefs, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", kbc.Dir, "for-each-ref",
   160	                                "--format=%(refname:short)", "refs/heads").CombinedOutput()
   161	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), string(outRefs))
   162	                        Expect(string(outRefs)).NotTo(ContainSubstring("tmp-ancestor"))
   163	                        Expect(string(outRefs)).NotTo(ContainSubstring("tmp-original"))
   164	                        Expect(string(outRefs)).NotTo(ContainSubstring("tmp-upgrade"))
   165	                        Expect(string(outRefs)).NotTo(ContainSubstring("tmp-merge"))
   166	                })
   167	
   168	                It("should update project from v4.5.2 to v4.7.0 with --force flag and create conflict markers", func() {
   169	                        By("modifying original Makefile to use CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION v0.17.3")
   170	                        modifyMakefileControllerTools(kbc.Dir, "v0.17.3")
   171	
   172	                        By("running alpha update with --force (default behavior is squash)")
   173	                        cmd := exec.Command(
   174	                                kbc.BinaryName, "alpha", "update",
   175	                                "--from-version", fromVersion,
   176	                                "--to-version", toVersionWithConflict,
   177	                                "--from-branch", "main",
   178	                                "--force",
   179	                        )
   180	                        cmd.Dir = kbc.Dir
   181	                        out, err := kbc.Run(cmd)
   182	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), string(out))
   183	
   184	                        By("checking that custom code is preserved")
   185	                        validateCustomCodePreservation(kbc.Dir)
   186	
   187	                        By("checking that conflict markers are present in the project files")
   188	                        Expect(hasConflictMarkers(kbc.Dir)).To(BeTrue())
   189	
   190	                        By("checking that go module is upgraded to expected versions")
   191	                        validateCommonGoModule(kbc.Dir)
   192	
   193	                        By("checking that Makefile is updated and has conflict between old and new versions in Makefile")
   194	                        makefilePath := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, "Makefile")
   195	                        content, err := os.ReadFile(makefilePath)
   196	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to read Makefile after update")
   197	                        makefileStr := string(content)
   198	
   199	                        // Should update to the new version
   200	                        Expect(makefileStr).To(ContainSubstring(`GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION ?= v2.1.6`))
   201	
   202	                        // The original project was scaffolded with v0.17.2 (from v4.5.2).
   203	                        // The user manually updated it to v0.17.3.
   204	                        // The target upgrade version (v4.7.0) introduces v0.18.0.
   205	                        //
   206	                        // Because both the user's version (v0.17.3) and the scaffold version (v0.18.0) differ,
   207	                        // we expect Git to insert conflict markers around this line in the Makefile:
   208	                        //
   209	                        // <<<<<<< HEAD
   210	                        // CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= v0.18.0
   211	                        // =======
   212	                        // CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= v0.17.3
   213	                        // >>>>>>> tmp-original-*
   214	                        Expect(makefileStr).To(ContainSubstring("<<<<<<<"),
   215	                                "Expected conflict marker <<<<<<< in Makefile")
   216	                        Expect(makefileStr).To(ContainSubstring("======="),
   217	                                "Expected conflict separator ======= in Makefile")
   218	                        Expect(makefileStr).To(ContainSubstring(">>>>>>>"),
   219	                                "Expected conflict marker >>>>>>> in Makefile")
   220	                        Expect(makefileStr).To(ContainSubstring("CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= v0.17.3"),
   221	                                "Expected original user version in conflict")
   222	                        Expect(makefileStr).To(ContainSubstring("CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= v0.18.0"),
   223	                                "Expected latest scaffold version in conflict")
   224	
   225	                        By("checking that the output branch (squashed) exists and is 1 commit ahead of main")
   226	                        prBranch := "kubebuilder-update-from-" + fromVersion + "-to-" + toVersionWithConflict
   227	
   228	                        git := func(args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
   229	                                cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
   230	                                cmd.Dir = kbc.Dir
   231	                                return cmd.CombinedOutput()
   232	                        }
   233	
   234	                        By("checking that the squashed branch exists")
   235	                        _, err = git("rev-parse", "--verify", prBranch)
   236	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   237	
   238	                        By("checking that exactly one squashed commit ahead of main")
   239	                        count, err := git("rev-list", "--count", prBranch, "^main")
   240	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), string(count))
   241	                        Expect(strings.TrimSpace(string(count))).To(Equal("1"))
   242	
   243	                        By("checking commit message of the squashed branch")
   244	                        msg, err := git("log", "-1", "--pretty=%B", prBranch)
   245	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), string(msg))
   246	                        expected := helpers.ConflictCommitMessage(fromVersion, toVersionWithConflict)
   247	                        Expect(string(msg)).To(ContainSubstring(expected))
   248	                })
   249	
   250	                It("should stop when updating the project from v4.5.2 to v4.7.0 without the flag force", func() {
   251	                        By("running alpha update without --force flag")
   252	                        cmd := exec.Command(
   253	                                kbc.BinaryName, "alpha", "update",
   254	                                "--from-version", fromVersion,
   255	                                "--to-version", toVersionWithConflict,
   256	                                "--from-branch", "main",
   257	                        )
   258	                        cmd.Dir = kbc.Dir
   259	                        out, err := kbc.Run(cmd)
   260	                        Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
   261	                        Expect(string(out)).To(ContainSubstring("merge stopped due to conflicts"))
   262	
   263	                        By("validating that merge stopped with conflicts requiring manual resolution")
   264	                        validateConflictState(kbc.Dir)
   265	
   266	                        By("checking that custom code is preserved")
   267	                        validateCustomCodePreservation(kbc.Dir)
   268	
   269	                        By("checking that go module is upgraded")
   270	                        validateCommonGoModule(kbc.Dir)
   271	                })
   272	
   273	                It("should preserve specified paths from base when squashing (e.g., .github/workflows)", func() {
   274	                        By("adding a workflow on main branch that should be preserved")
   275	                        wfDir := filepath.Join(kbc.Dir, ".github", "workflows")
   276	                        Expect(os.MkdirAll(wfDir, 0o755)).To(Succeed())
   277	                        wf := filepath.Join(wfDir, "ci.yml")
   278	                        Expect(os.WriteFile(wf, []byte("name: KEEP_ME\n"), 0o644)).To(Succeed())
   279	
   280	                        git := func(args ...string) {
   281	                                c := exec.Command("git", args...)
   282	                                c.Dir = kbc.Dir
   283	                                o, e := c.CombinedOutput()
   284	                                Expect(e).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), string(o))
   285	                        }
   286	                        git("add", ".github/workflows/ci.yml")
   287	                        git("commit", "-m", "add ci workflow")
   288	
   289	                        By("running update (default squash) with --restore-path")
   290	                        cmd := exec.Command(
   291	                                kbc.BinaryName, "alpha", "update",
   292	                                "--from-version", fromVersion,
   293	                                "--to-version", toVersion,
   294	                                "--from-branch", "main",
   295	                                "--restore-path", ".github/workflows",
   296	                        )
   297	                        cmd.Dir = kbc.Dir
   298	                        out, err := kbc.Run(cmd)
   299	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), string(out))
   300	
   301	                        By("workflow content is preserved on output branch")
   302	                        data, err := os.ReadFile(wf)
   303	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   304	                        Expect(string(data)).To(ContainSubstring("KEEP_ME"))
   305	                })
   306	
   307	                It("should succeed with no action when from-version and to-version are the same", func() {
   308	                        cmd := exec.Command(kbc.BinaryName, "alpha", "update",
   309	                                "--from-version", fromVersion,
   310	                                "--to-version", fromVersion,
   311	                                "--from-branch", "main")
   312	                        output, err := kbc.Run(cmd)
   313	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   314	                        Expect(string(output)).To(ContainSubstring("already uses the specified version"))
   315	                        Expect(string(output)).To(ContainSubstring("No action taken"))
   316	                })
   317	        })

    37	// Variables and function to check Go version requirements.
    38	var (
    39	        goVerMin = golang.MustParse("go1.23.0")
    40	        goVerMax = golang.MustParse("go2.0alpha1")
    41	)
    42	
    43	var _ plugin.InitSubcommand = &initSubcommand{}
    44	
    45	type initSubcommand struct {
    46	        config config.Config
    47	        // For help text.
    48	        commandName string
    49	
    50	        // boilerplate options
    51	        license string
    52	        owner   string
    53	
    54	        // go config options
    55	        repo string
    56	
    57	        // flags
    58	        fetchDeps          bool
    59	        skipGoVersionCheck bool
    60	        multigroup         bool
    61	        namespaced         bool
    62	}
    63	
    64	func (p *initSubcommand) UpdateMetadata(cliMeta plugin.CLIMetadata, subcmdMeta *plugin.SubcommandMetadata) {
    65	        p.commandName = cliMeta.CommandName
    66	
    67	        subcmdMeta.Description = `Initialize a new project including the following files:
    68	  - a "go.mod" with project dependencies
    69	  - a "PROJECT" file that stores project configuration
    70	  - a "Makefile" with several useful make targets for the project
    71	  - several YAML files for project deployment under the "config" directory
    72	  - a "cmd/main.go" file that creates the manager that will run the project controllers
    73	
    74	Required flags:
    75	  --domain: Domain for your APIs (e.g., example.org creates crew.example.org for API groups)
    76	
    77	Configuration flags:
    78	  --repo: Go module path (e.g., github.com/user/repo); auto-detected if not provided
    79	  --owner: Owner name for copyright license headers
    80	  --license: License to use (apache2 or none, default: apache2)
    81	
    82	Plugin flags:
    83	  --plugins: Comma-separated list of plugins to use (default: go/v4)
    84	             Plugins scaffold files during init and are saved to the PROJECT layout
    85	             Future operations (i.e. create api, create webhook) call all plugins in the chain
    86	             Run 'kubebuilder init --plugins --help' to see available plugins
    87	
    88	Layout flags:
    89	  --multigroup: Enable multigroup layout to organize APIs by group
    90	                Scaffolds APIs in api/<group>/<version>/ instead of api/<version>/
    91	                Useful when managing multiple API groups (e.g., batch, apps, crew)
    92	  --namespaced: Enable namespace-scoped deployment instead of cluster-scoped
    93	                Manager watches one or more specific namespaces instead of all namespaces
    94	                Namespaces to watch are configured via WATCH_NAMESPACE environment variable
    95	                Uses Role/RoleBinding instead of ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding
    96	                Suitable for multi-tenant environments or limited scope deployments
    97	
    98	Note: Layout settings can be changed later with 'kubebuilder edit'.
    99	`
   100	        subcmdMeta.Examples = fmt.Sprintf(`  # Initialize a new project
   101	  %[1]s init --domain example.org
   102	
   103	  # Initialize with multigroup layout
   104	  %[1]s init --domain example.org --multigroup
   105	
   106	  # Initialize with namespace-scoped deployment
   107	  %[1]s init --domain example.org --namespaced
   108	
   109	  # Initialize with optional plugins
   110	  %[1]s init --plugins go/v4,autoupdate/v1-alpha --domain example.org
   111	  %[1]s init --plugins go/v4,helm/v2-alpha --domain example.org
   112	
   113	  # Initialize with custom settings
   114	  %[1]s init --domain example.org --owner "Your Name" --license apache2
   115	
   116	  # Initialize with all options combined
   117	  %[1]s init --plugins go/v4,autoupdate/v1-alpha --domain example.org --multigroup --namespaced
   118	`, cliMeta.CommandName)
   119	}
   120	
   121	func (p *initSubcommand) BindFlags(fs *pflag.FlagSet) {
   122	        fs.BoolVar(&p.skipGoVersionCheck, "skip-go-version-check",
   123	                false, "skip Go version check")
   124	
   125	        // dependency args
   126	        fs.BoolVar(&p.fetchDeps, "fetch-deps", true, "download dependencies after scaffolding")
   127	
   128	        // boilerplate args
   129	        fs.StringVar(&p.license, "license", "apache2",
   130	                "license header to use (apache2 or none)")
   131	        fs.StringVar(&p.owner, "owner", "", "copyright owner for license headers")
   132	
   133	        // project args
   134	        fs.StringVar(&p.repo, "repo", "", "Go module name (e.g., github.com/user/repo); "+
   135	                "auto-detected from current directory if not provided")
   136	        fs.BoolVar(&p.multigroup, "multigroup", false,
   137	                "enable multigroup layout (organize APIs by group)")
   138	        fs.BoolVar(&p.namespaced, "namespaced", false,
   139	                "enable namespace-scoped deployment (default: cluster-scoped)")
   140	}
   141	
   142	func (p *initSubcommand) InjectConfig(c config.Config) error {
   143	        p.config = c
   144	
   145	        // Try to guess repository if flag is not set.
   146	        if p.repo == "" {
   147	                repoPath, err := golang.FindCurrentRepo()
   148	                if err != nil {
   149	                        return fmt.Errorf("error finding current repository: %w", err)
   150	                }
   151	                p.repo = repoPath
   152	        }
   153	
   154	        if err := p.config.SetRepository(p.repo); err != nil {
   155	                return fmt.Errorf("error setting repository: %w", err)
   156	        }
   157	
   158	        if p.multigroup {
   159	                if err := p.config.SetMultiGroup(); err != nil {
   160	                        return fmt.Errorf("error setting multigroup: %w", err)
   161	                }
   162	        }
   163	
   164	        if p.namespaced {
   165	                if err := p.config.SetNamespaced(); err != nil {
   166	                        return fmt.Errorf("error setting namespaced: %w", err)
   167	                }
   168	        }
   169	
   170	        return nil
   171	}
   172	
   173	func (p *initSubcommand) PreScaffold(machinery.Filesystem) error {
   174	        // Ensure Go version is in the allowed range if check not turned off.
   175	        if !p.skipGoVersionCheck {
   176	                if err := golang.ValidateGoVersion(goVerMin, goVerMax); err != nil {
   177	                        return fmt.Errorf("error validating go version: %w", err)
   178	                }
   179	        }
   180	
   181	        // Check if the current directory has no files or directories which does not allow to init the project
   182	        return checkDir()
   183	}
   184	
   185	func (p *initSubcommand) Scaffold(fs machinery.Filesystem) error {
   186	        scaffolder := scaffolds.NewInitScaffolder(p.config, p.license, p.owner, p.commandName)
   187	        scaffolder.InjectFS(fs)
   188	        if err := scaffolder.Scaffold(); err != nil {
   189	                return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding init plugin: %w", err)
   190	        }
   191	
   192	        if !p.fetchDeps {
   193	                log.Info("skipping fetching dependencies")
   194	                return nil
   195	        }
   196	
   197	        // Ensure that we are pinning controller-runtime version
   198	        // xref: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kubebuilder/issues/997
   199	        err := util.RunCmd("Get controller runtime", "go", "get",
   200	                "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime@"+scaffolds.ControllerRuntimeVersion)
   201	        if err != nil {
   202	                return fmt.Errorf("error getting controller-runtime version: %w", err)
   203	        }
   204	
   205	        return nil
   206	}
   207	
   208	func (p *initSubcommand) PostScaffold() error {
   209	        err := util.RunCmd("Update dependencies", "go", "mod", "tidy")
   210	        if err != nil {
   211	                return fmt.Errorf("error updating go dependencies: %w", err)
   212	        }
   213	
   214	        fmt.Printf("Next: define a resource with:\n$ %s create api\n", p.commandName)
   215	        return nil
   216	}
   217	
   218	// checkDir checks the target directory before scaffolding:
   219	// 1. Returns error if key kubebuilder files already exist (prevents re-initialization)
   220	// 2. Warns if directory is not empty (but allows scaffolding to continue)
   221	func checkDir() error {
   222	        // Files scaffolded by 'kubebuilder init' that indicate the directory is already initialized.
   223	        // Blocking these prevents accidental re-initialization and file conflicts.
   224	        // Note: go.mod and go.sum are NOT blocked because:
   225	        //   - They may exist in pre-existing Go projects
   226	        //   - Kubebuilder will overwrite them (machinery.OverwriteFile)
   227	        //   - Testdata generation creates go.mod before running init
   228	        scaffoldedFiles := []string{
   229	                "PROJECT",                       // Kubebuilder project config (key indicator)
   230	                "Makefile",                      // Build automation
   231	                filepath.Join("cmd", "main.go"), // Controller manager entry point
   232	        }
   233	
   234	        // Check for existing scaffolded files
   235	        for _, file := range scaffoldedFiles {
   236	                if _, err := os.Stat(file); err == nil {
   237	                        return fmt.Errorf("target directory is already initialized. "+
   238	                                "Found existing kubebuilder file %q. "+
   239	                                "Please run this command in a new directory or remove existing scaffolded files", file)
   240	                }
   241	        }
   242	
   243	        // Check if directory has any other files (warn only)
   244	        // Note: We ignore certain files that are expected or safely overwritten:
   245	        //   - go.mod and go.sum: Users may run `go mod init` before `kubebuilder init`
   246	        //   - .gitignore and .dockerignore: Safely overwritten by kubebuilder
   247	        //   - Other dot directories (.git, .vscode, .idea): Not scaffolded by kubebuilder
   248	        // However, we DO check .github directory since kubebuilder scaffolds workflows there
   249	        var hasFiles bool
   250	        err := filepath.Walk(".",
   251	                func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
   252	                        if err != nil {
   253	                                return fmt.Errorf("error walking path %q: %w", path, err)
   254	                        }
   255	                        // Skip the current directory itself
   256	                        if path == "." {
   257	                                return nil
   258	                        }
   259	                        // Skip dot directories EXCEPT .github (which contains scaffolded workflows)
   260	                        if info.IsDir() && strings.HasPrefix(info.Name(), ".") {
   261	                                if info.Name() != ".github" {
   262	                                        return filepath.SkipDir
   263	                                }
   264	                        }
   265	                        // Skip files that are expected or safely overwritten
   266	                        ignoredFiles := []string{"go.mod", "go.sum"}
   267	                        if slices.Contains(ignoredFiles, info.Name()) {
   268	                                return nil
   269	                        }
   270	                        // Track if any other files/directories exist
   271	                        hasFiles = true
   272	                        return nil
   273	                })
   274	        if err != nil {
   275	                return fmt.Errorf("error walking directory: %w", err)
   276	        }
   277	
   278	        // Warn if directory is not empty (but don't block)
   279	        if hasFiles {
   280	                log.Warn("The target directory is not empty. " +
   281	                        "Scaffolding may overwrite existing files or cause conflicts. " +
   282	                        "It is recommended to initialize in an empty directory.")
   283	        }
   284	
   285	        return nil
   286	}
   287

// ReplaceBoilerplateYear replaces the YEAR placeholder in the boilerplate with the current year.
// This matches the behavior of both gengo and controller-gen, which substitute YEAR
// with the current year at code-generation time.
func ReplaceBoilerplateYear(boilerplate string) string {
        return strings.ReplaceAll(boilerplate, "YEAR", fmt.Sprintf("%v", time.Now().Year()))

func WithBoilerplate(boilerplate string) ScaffoldOption {
        return func(s *Scaffold) {
                s.injector.boilerplate = boilerplate
        }

// ReplaceBoilerplateYear replaces the YEAR placeholder in the boilerplate with the current year.
// This matches the behavior of both gengo and controller-gen, which substitute YEAR
// with the current year at code-generation time.
func ReplaceBoilerplateYear(boilerplate string) string {
        return strings.ReplaceAll(boilerplate, "YEAR", fmt.Sprintf("%v", time.Now().Year()))
}'

    17	package hack
    18	
    19	import (
    20	        "fmt"
    21	        "path/filepath"
    22	        "time"
    23	
    24	        "sigs.k8s.io/kubebuilder/v4/pkg/machinery"
    25	)
    26	
    27	// DefaultBoilerplatePath is the default path to the boilerplate file
    28	var DefaultBoilerplatePath = filepath.Join("hack", "boilerplate.go.txt")
    29	
    30	var _ machinery.Template = &Boilerplate{}
    31	
    32	// Boilerplate scaffolds a file that defines the common header for the rest of the files
    33	type Boilerplate struct {
    34	        machinery.TemplateMixin
    35	        machinery.BoilerplateMixin
    36	
    37	        // License is the License type to write
    38	        License string
    39	
    40	        // Licenses maps License types to their actual string
    41	        Licenses map[string]string
    42	
    43	        // Owner is the copyright owner - e.g. "The Kubernetes Authors"
    44	        Owner string
    45	
    46	        // Year is the copyright year
    47	        Year string
    48	}
    49	
    50	// Validate implements file.RequiresValidation
    51	func (f *Boilerplate) Validate() error {
    52	        if f.License != "" {
    53	                if _, foundKnown := knownLicenses[f.License]; !foundKnown {
    54	                        if _, found := f.Licenses[f.License]; !found {
    55	                                return fmt.Errorf("unknown specified license %s", f.License)
    56	                        }
    57	                }
    58	        }
    59	        return nil
    60	}
    61	
    62	// SetTemplateDefaults implements machinery.Template
    63	func (f *Boilerplate) SetTemplateDefaults() error {
    64	        if f.Path == "" {
    65	                f.Path = DefaultBoilerplatePath
    66	        }
    67	
    68	        if f.License == "" {
    69	                f.License = "apache2"
    70	        }
    71	
    72	        if f.Licenses == nil {
    73	                f.Licenses = make(map[string]string, len(knownLicenses))
    74	        }
    75	
    76	        for key, value := range knownLicenses {
    77	                if _, hasLicense := f.Licenses[key]; !hasLicense {
    78	                        f.Licenses[key] = value
    79	                }
    80	        }
    81	
    82	        if f.Year == "" {
    83	                f.Year = "YEAR"
    84	        }
    85	
    86	        // Boilerplate given
    87	        if len(f.Boilerplate) > 0 {
    88	                f.TemplateBody = f.Boilerplate
    89	                return nil
    90	        }
    91	
    92	        f.TemplateBody = boilerplateTemplate
    93	
    94	        return nil
    95	}
    96	
    97	const boilerplateTemplate = `/*
    98	{{ if .Owner -}}
    99	Copyright {{ .Year }} {{ .Owner }}.
   100	{{- else -}}
   101	Copyright {{ .Year }}.
   102	{{- end }}
   103	{{ index .Licenses .License }}*/`
   104	
   105	var knownLicenses = map[string]string{
   106	        "apache2":   apache2,
   107	        "copyright": "",
   108	}
   109	
   110	const apache2 = `
   111	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
   112	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
   113	You may obtain a copy of the License at
   114	
   115	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
   116	
   117	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
   118	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
   119	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
   120	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
   121	limitations under the License.
   122	`
   123

+                                return err

+                }
+        }
+

+                return SetTemplateDefaultsError{err}
+        }
+
+        path := t.GetPath()
+
+        // Handle already existing models
+        if _, found := models[path]; found {
+                switch t.GetIfExistsAction() {
+                case SkipFile:
+                        return nil
+                case Error:
+                        return ModelAlreadyExistsError{path}
+                case OverwriteFile:
+                default:
+                        return UnknownIfExistsActionError{path, t.GetIfExistsAction()}
+                }
+        }
+
+        b, err := doTemplate(t)
+        if err != nil {
+                return err
+        }
+
+        models[path] = &File{
+                Path:           path,
+                Contents:       string(b),
+                IfExistsAction: t.GetIfExistsAction(),
+        }
+        return nil
 }

+        leftDelim, rightDelim := t.GetDelim()
+        if leftDelim != "" && rightDelim != "" {
+                temp.Delims(leftDelim, rightDelim)
+        }
+
+        // Set the function map to be used
+        fm := DefaultFuncMap()
+        if templateWithFuncMap, hasCustomFuncMap := t.(UseCustomFuncMap); hasCustomFuncMap {
+                fm = templateWithFuncMap.GetFuncMap()
+        }
+        temp.Funcs(fm)
+

+        if _, err := temp.Parse(t.GetBody()); err != nil {
+                return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse template: %w", err)
+        }
+
+        // Execute the template
+        out := &bytes.Buffer{}
+        if err := temp.Execute(out, t); err != nil {
+                return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute template: %w", err)
+        }
+        b := out.Bytes()
+
+        // TODO(adirio): move go-formatting to write step
+        // gofmt the imports
+        if filepath.Ext(t.GetPath()) == ".go" {
+                var err error
+                if b, err = imports.Process(t.GetPath(), b, &options); err != nil {
+                        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to process template: %w", err)
+                }
+        }
+
+        return b, nil
 }

-	// Remove code fragments that already were applied
-	err = filterExistingValues(m.Contents, codeFragments)

+        m, err := s.loadPreviousModel(i, models)
+        if err != nil {
+                if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
+                        if withOptionalBehavior, ok := i.(HasIfNotExistsAction); ok {
+                                switch withOptionalBehavior.GetIfNotExistsAction() {
+                                case IgnoreFile:
+                                        log.Warn("skipping missing file", "file", i.GetPath())
+                                        log.Warn("the code fragments will not be inserted")
+                                        return nil
+                                case ErrorIfNotExist:
+                                        return err
+                                default:
+                                        return err
+                                }
+                        }
+                        // If inserter doesn't implement HasIfNotExistsAction, return the original error
+                        return err
+                }
+                return fmt.Errorf("failed to load previous model for %s: %w", i.GetPath(), err)
+        }
+
+        // Get valid code fragments
+        codeFragments := getValidCodeFragments(i)
+
+        // Remove code fragments that already were applied
+        err = filterExistingValues(m.Contents, codeFragments)
+        if err != nil {
+                return fmt.Errorf("failed to filter existing values: %w", err)
+        }
+
+        // If no code fragment to insert, we are done
+        if len(codeFragments) == 0 {
+                return nil
+        }
+
+        content, err := insertStrings(m.Contents, codeFragments)
+        if err != nil {
+                return fmt.Errorf("failed to insert values: %w", err)
+        }
+
+        // TODO(adirio): move go-formatting to write step
+        formattedContent := content
+        if ext := filepath.Ext(i.GetPath()); ext == ".go" {
+                formattedContent, err = imports.Process(i.GetPath(), content, nil)
+                if err != nil {
+                        return fmt.Errorf("failed to process formatted content: %w", err)
+                }
+        }
+
+        m.Contents = string(formattedContent)
+        m.IfExistsAction = OverwriteFile
+        models[m.Path] = m
+        return nil
 }

-			// Model has preference

-			return nil, UnknownIfExistsActionError{path, m.IfExistsAction}

+        path := i.GetPath()
+
+        // Let's see if we already have a model for this file
+        if m, found := models[path]; found {
+                // Check if there is already a scaffolded file
+                exists, err := afero.Exists(s.fs, path)
+                if err != nil {
+                        return nil, ExistsFileError{err}
+                }
+
+                // If there is a model but no scaffolded file we return the model
+                if !exists {
+                        return m, nil
+                }
+
+                // If both a model and a file are found, check which has preference
+                switch m.IfExistsAction {
+                case SkipFile:
+                        // File has preference
+                        fromFile, err := s.loadModelFromFile(path)
+                        if err != nil {
+                                return m, nil
+                        }
+                        return fromFile, nil
+                case Error:
+                        // Writing will result in an error, so we can return error now
+                        return nil, FileAlreadyExistsError{path}
+                case OverwriteFile:
+                        // Model has preference
+                        return m, nil
+                default:
+                        return nil, UnknownIfExistsActionError{path, m.IfExistsAction}
+                }
+        }
+
+        // There was no model
+        return s.loadModelFromFile(path)
 }

 func (s Scaffold) loadModelFromFile(path string) (f *File, err error) {

+        reader, err := s.fs.Open(path)
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, OpenFileError{err}
+        }
+        defer func() {
+                if closeErr := reader.Close(); err == nil && closeErr != nil {
+                        err = CloseFileError{closeErr}
+                }
+        }()
+
+        content, err := afero.ReadAll(reader)
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, ReadFileError{err}
+        }
+
+        return &File{Path: path, Contents: string(content)}, nil
 }

+                        exists = true

+                return false, scanMultilineErr
+        }
+
+        return exists, nil

 // scanMultiline scans a string while buffering the specified number of scanLines. It calls scanFunc
 // for every group of lines. The content passed to scanFunc will have trimmed whitespace. It
 // continues scanning the content as long as scanFunc returns true.
 func scanMultiline(content string, scanLines int, scanFunc func(contentGroup string) bool) error {
-	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(strings.NewReader(content))

-				}
-
-				return nil
-			}
-		}
-
-		if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
-			return fmt.Errorf("failed to scan content: %w", err)
-		}
-
-		return nil
-	}
-
-	// Complex case.
-	bufferedLines := make([]string, scanLines)
-	bufferedLinesIndex := 0
-	var sb strings.Builder
-
-	for scanner.Scan() {
-		// Trim space on each line in order to match different levels of indentation.
-		bufferedLines[bufferedLinesIndex] = strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())

-		for i := range scanLines {
-			_, _ = sb.WriteString(bufferedLines[(bufferedLinesIndex+i)%scanLines])
-			_ = sb.WriteByte('\n')

-			if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {

-			}

-			return nil
-		}

-

-		return fmt.Errorf("failed to scan content: %w", err)
-	}
-
-	return nil
+        scanner := bufio.NewScanner(strings.NewReader(content))
+
+        // Optimized simple case.
+        if scanLines == 1 {
+                for scanner.Scan() {
+                        if !scanFunc(strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())) {
+                                if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
+                                        return fmt.Errorf("failed to scan content: %w", err)
+                                }
+
+                                return nil
+                        }
+                }
+
+                if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
+                        return fmt.Errorf("failed to scan content: %w", err)
+                }
+
+                return nil
+        }
+
+        // Complex case.
+        bufferedLines := make([]string, scanLines)
+        bufferedLinesIndex := 0
+        var sb strings.Builder
+
+        for scanner.Scan() {
+                // Trim space on each line in order to match different levels of indentation.
+                bufferedLines[bufferedLinesIndex] = strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
+                bufferedLinesIndex = (bufferedLinesIndex + 1) % scanLines
+
+                sb.Reset()
+                for i := range scanLines {
+                        _, _ = sb.WriteString(bufferedLines[(bufferedLinesIndex+i)%scanLines])
+                        _ = sb.WriteByte('\n')
+                }
+
+                if !scanFunc(strings.TrimSpace(sb.String())) {
+                        if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
+                                return fmt.Errorf("failed to scan content: %w", err)
+                        }
+
+                        return nil
+                }
+        }
+
+        if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
+                return fmt.Errorf("failed to scan content: %w", err)
+        }
+
+        return nil
 }
 
 func insertStrings(content string, codeFragmentsMap CodeFragmentsMap) ([]byte, error) {

+        out := new(bytes.Buffer)
+
+        scanner := bufio.NewScanner(strings.NewReader(content))
+        for scanner.Scan() {
+                line := scanner.Text()
+
+                for marker, codeFragments := range codeFragmentsMap {
+                        if marker.EqualsLine(line) {
+                                for _, codeFragment := range codeFragments {
+                                        _, _ = out.WriteString(codeFragment) // bytes.Buffer.WriteString always returns nil errors
+                                }
+                        }
+                }
+
+                _, _ = out.WriteString(line + "\n") // bytes.Buffer.WriteString always returns nil errors
+        }
+        if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
+                return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan content: %w", err)
+        }
+
+        return out.Bytes(), nil
 }
 
 func (s Scaffold) writeFile(f *File) error {
-	// Check if the file to write already exists
-	exists, err := afero.Exists(s.fs, f.Path)
-	if err != nil {
-		return ExistsFileError{err}
-	}
-	if exists {
-		switch f.IfExistsAction {
-		case OverwriteFile:
-			// By not returning, the file is written as if it didn't exist
-		case SkipFile:
-			// By returning nil, the file is not written but the process will carry on
-			return nil
-		case Error:
-			// By returning an error, the file is not written and the process will fail
-			return FileAlreadyExistsError{f.Path}
-		}
-	}
-
-	// Create the directory if needed
-	if err = s.fs.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(f.Path), s.dirPerm); err != nil {
-		return CreateDirectoryError{err}
-	}
-
-	// Create or truncate the file
-	writer, err := s.fs.OpenFile(f.Path, createOrUpdate, s.filePerm)
-	if err != nil {
-		return CreateFileError{err}
-	}
-	defer func() {
-		if closeErr := writer.Close(); err == nil && closeErr != nil {
-			err = CloseFileError{err}
-		}
-	}()
-
-	if _, writeErr := writer.Write([]byte(f.Contents)); writeErr != nil {
-		return WriteFileError{writeErr}
-	}
-
-	return nil
+        // Check if the file to write already exists
+        exists, err := afero.Exists(s.fs, f.Path)
+        if err != nil {
+                return ExistsFileError{err}
+        }
+        if exists {
+                switch f.IfExistsAction {
+                case OverwriteFile:
+                        // By not returning, the file is written as if it didn't exist
+                case SkipFile:
+                        // By returning nil, the file is not written but the process will carry on
+                        return nil
+                case Error:
+                        // By returning an error, the file is not written and the process will fail
+                        return FileAlreadyExistsError{f.Path}
+                }
+        }
+
+        // Create the directory if needed
+        if err = s.fs.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(f.Path), s.dirPerm); err != nil {
+                return CreateDirectoryError{err}
+        }
+
+        // Create or truncate the file
+        writer, err := s.fs.OpenFile(f.Path, createOrUpdate, s.filePerm)
+        if err != nil {
+                return CreateFileError{err}
+        }
+        defer func() {
+                if closeErr := writer.Close(); err == nil && closeErr != nil {
+                        err = CloseFileError{err}
+                }
+        }()
+
+        if _, writeErr := writer.Write([]byte(f.Contents)); writeErr != nil {
+                return WriteFileError{writeErr}
+        }
+
+        return nil
 }

 func (s *apiScaffolder) Scaffold() error {

-			&controllers.ControllerTest{Force: s.force, DoAPI: doAPI},
-		); err != nil {
-			return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding controller: %w", err)
-		}
-	}
-
-	if err := scaffold.Execute(
-		&cmd.MainUpdater{WireResource: doAPI, WireController: doController},
-	); err != nil {
-		return fmt.Errorf("error updating cmd/main.go: %w", err)
-	}
-
-	return nil
+        log.Info("Writing scaffold for you to edit...")
+
+        // Load the boilerplate
+        boilerplate, err := afero.ReadFile(s.fs.FS, hack.DefaultBoilerplatePath)
+        if err != nil {
+                if errors.Is(err, afero.ErrFileNotFound) {
+                        log.Warn("unable to find boilerplate file. "+
+                                "This file is used to generate the license header in the project.\n"+
+                                "Note that controller-gen will also use this. Ensure that you "+
+                                "add the license file or configure your project accordingly",
+                                "file_path", hack.DefaultBoilerplatePath, "error", err)
+                        boilerplate = []byte("")
+                } else {
+                        return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding API/controller: failed to load boilerplate: %w", err)
+                }
+        }
+
+        // Replace YEAR placeholder with the current year
+        boilerplateStr := machinery.ReplaceBoilerplateYear(string(boilerplate))
+
+        // Initialize the machinery.Scaffold that will write the files to disk
+        scaffold := machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
+                machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
+                machinery.WithBoilerplate(boilerplateStr),
+                machinery.WithResource(&s.resource),
+        )
+
+        // Keep track of these values before the update
+        doAPI := s.resource.HasAPI()
+        doController := s.resource.HasController()
+
+        if err := s.config.UpdateResource(s.resource); err != nil {
+                return fmt.Errorf("error updating resource: %w", err)
+        }
+
+        if doAPI {
+                if err := scaffold.Execute(
+                        &api.Types{Force: s.force},
+                        &api.Group{},
+                ); err != nil {
+                        return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding APIs: %w", err)
+                }
+        }
+
+        if doController {
+                if err := scaffold.Execute(
+                        &controllers.SuiteTest{Force: s.force},
+                        &controllers.Controller{ControllerRuntimeVersion: ControllerRuntimeVersion, Force: s.force},
+                        &controllers.ControllerTest{Force: s.force, DoAPI: doAPI},
+                ); err != nil {
+                        return fmt.Errorf("error scaffolding controller: %w", err)
+                }
+        }
+
+        if err := scaffold.Execute(
+                &cmd.MainUpdater{WireResource: doAPI, WireController: doController},
+        ); err != nil {
+                return fmt.Errorf("error updating cmd/main.go: %w", err)
+        }
+
+        return nil
 }

+                if err := scaffold.Execute(bpFile); err != nil {
+                        return fmt.Errorf("failed to execute boilerplate: %w", err)
+                }

+                boilerplate, err := afero.ReadFile(s.fs.FS, s.boilerplatePath)
+                if err != nil {
+                        if errors.Is(err, afero.ErrFileNotFound) {
+                                log.Warn("unable to find boilerplate file. "+
+                                        "This file is used to generate the license header in the project.\n"+
+                                        "Note that controller-gen will also use this. Ensure that you "+
+                                        "add the license file or configure your project accordingly",
+                                        "file_path", s.boilerplatePath,
+                                        "error", err)
+                                boilerplate = []byte("")
+                        } else {
+                                return fmt.Errorf("failed to load boilerplate: %w", err)
+                        }
+                }
+                // Replace YEAR placeholder with the current year
+                boilerplateStr := machinery.ReplaceBoilerplateYear(string(boilerplate))
+                // Initialize the machinery.Scaffold that will write the files to disk
+                scaffold = machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
+                        machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
+                        machinery.WithBoilerplate(boilerplateStr),
+                )
+        } else {
+                s.boilerplatePath = ""
+                // Initialize the machinery.Scaffold without boilerplate
+                scaffold = machinery.NewScaffold(s.fs,
+                        machinery.WithConfig(s.config),
+                )
+        }

+        // we need to ensure that we use its supported Kustomize Version

+        gov4 := "go.kubebuilder.io/v4"

+

+                if pluginKey == pluginKeyForKustomizeV2 || pluginKey == gov4 {
+                        kustomizeVersion = kustomizecommonv2.KustomizeVersion
+                        break
+                }
+        }
+

+                &cmd.Main{
+                        ControllerRuntimeVersion: ControllerRuntimeVersion,
+                },
+                &templates.GoMod{
+                        ControllerRuntimeVersion: ControllerRuntimeVersion,
+                },
+                &templates.GitIgnore{},
+                &templates.Makefile{

+                        BoilerplatePath:          s.boilerplatePath,
+                        ControllerToolsVersion:   ControllerToolsVersion,
+                        KustomizeVersion:         kustomizeVersion,
+                        GolangciLintVersion:      GolangciLintVersion,

+                        EnvtestVersion:           getControllerRuntimeReleaseBranch(),

-	// approach, although not ideal, remains the best solution for ensuring
-	// compatibility with controller-runtime releases as of now. For more

+        // change over time, potentially leading to compatibility issues. This
+        // approach, although not ideal, remains the best solution for ensuring
+        // compatibility with controller-runtime releases as of now. For more
+        // details on the quest for a more robust solution, refer to the issue
+        // raised in the controller-runtime repository: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/issues/2744
